#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Tier-2 round-trip e2e: cross-simulator memory survival via shared mmap.
#
# QEMU 1, vsim, and QEMU 2 all map the same backing file. Phase A writes a
# pattern in QEMU 1; Phase B verifies + mutates in vsim; Phase C re-reads
# in QEMU 2. Memory must survive both handoffs.
#
# Required environment:
#   VSIM_BIN     path to sim_<cpu> binary (host-runnable)
#   VSIM_QEMU    path to qemu-system-riscv64
#   VSIM_PLUGIN  path to libhybrid_handoff.so

set -euo pipefail

SCRIPT_DIR=$(cd -- "$(dirname -- "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)
REPO_ROOT=$(cd -- "$SCRIPT_DIR/../.." && pwd)

# Fixture-parameterized: defaults match the original rt_shared_mem.S/elf
# fixture, but C-level Tier-2 fixtures (rt_c_hello, rt_c_printf, ...)
# reuse this harness by overriding FIXTURE/EXPECT_RC.
FIXTURE="${FIXTURE:-rt_shared_mem}"
EXPECT_RC="${EXPECT_RC:-0}"
TIMEOUT="${TIMEOUT:-60}"
TRIGGER_MODE="${TRIGGER_MODE:-csr}"

VSIM_BIN="${VSIM_BIN:-$REPO_ROOT/build/sim_ax45mpv_premium}"
VSIM_QEMU="${VSIM_QEMU:-/local/nick/qemu_v5/build/qemu-system-riscv64}"
VSIM_PLUGIN="${VSIM_PLUGIN:-$REPO_ROOT/hybrid/qemu_plugin/libhybrid_handoff.so}"
VSIM_ELF="${VSIM_ELF:-$REPO_ROOT/hybrid/test/${FIXTURE}.elf}"
NM="${NM:-/local/nick/SW_Release/build-ast540/build-toolchain/linux/nds64le-elf-newlib-v5d/bin/riscv64-unknown-elf-nm}"

for f in "$VSIM_BIN" "$VSIM_QEMU" "$VSIM_PLUGIN" "$VSIM_ELF"; do
  if [ ! -e "$f" ]; then
    echo "skip: $f not built; run 'make' under hybrid/test and hybrid/qemu_plugin"
    exit 77
  fi
done

case "$TRIGGER_MODE" in
  csr) ;;
  pc)
    if ! command -v "$NM" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
      echo "skip[$FIXTURE pc]: nm not found at $NM"
      exit 77
    fi
    # Capture nm output once: avoids racing awk's `exit` against nm's pipe
    # writes (large C-fixture symbol tables make nm SIGPIPE under pipefail).
    NM_OUT=$("$NM" --defined-only "$VSIM_ELF")
    ENTER_PC=$(printf '%s\n' "$NM_OUT" \
               | awk '$3=="_hybrid_enter_pc"{print $1}')
    EXIT_PC=$(printf '%s\n' "$NM_OUT" \
              | awk '$3=="_hybrid_exit_pc"{print $1}')
    if [ -z "${ENTER_PC:-}" ] || [ -z "${EXIT_PC:-}" ]; then
      echo "FAIL[$FIXTURE pc]: missing _hybrid_enter_pc/_hybrid_exit_pc symbols"
      exit 1
    fi
    ;;
  *)
    echo "FAIL[$FIXTURE]: unknown TRIGGER_MODE=$TRIGGER_MODE (use csr|pc)"
    exit 1
    ;;
esac

# 128 MB matches the QEMU virt machine default; vsim must use the same size.
SHARED_SIZE=$((128 * 1024 * 1024))
SHARED_MEM=$(mktemp -p /dev/shm vsim-rt-shared.XXXXXX)
STATE_QEMU=$(mktemp)
STATE_VSIM=$(mktemp)
SIM_LOG=$(mktemp)
trap 'rm -f "$SHARED_MEM" "$STATE_QEMU" "$STATE_VSIM" "$SIM_LOG"' EXIT

truncate -s "$SHARED_SIZE" "$SHARED_MEM"

PLUGIN_ARGS="outfile=$STATE_QEMU"
VSIM_TRIG_ARGS=()
if [ "$TRIGGER_MODE" = "pc" ]; then
  PLUGIN_ARGS="$PLUGIN_ARGS,enter_pc=0x$ENTER_PC"
  VSIM_TRIG_ARGS=(--hybrid-exit-pc "0x$EXIT_PC")
fi

echo "[$FIXTURE 1/3 mode=$TRIGGER_MODE] QEMU 1 (shared mmap): drain entry state"
# -cpu andes-ax45mpv,vlen=512: gates Andes-specific CSRs (mcache_ctl,
# mmisc_ctl, ...) and pins VLEN to 512 (vlenb=64) so the V state in the
# drain file matches HYBRID_VLEN_BYTES_MAX. Non-V fixtures run identically
# under either VLEN.
# -semihosting-config enable=on,target=native: lets fixtures print from
# QEMU1 (phase 1) via newlib stdio (linked against libgloss_vh), matching
# the QEMU2 launch in qemu_handback.hpp. Phase 2 (vsim) is forbidden from
# doing any syscall -- vsim does not implement semihosting.
"$VSIM_QEMU" \
  -object "memory-backend-file,id=mem,share=on,mem-path=$SHARED_MEM,size=$SHARED_SIZE" \
  -machine andes_ae350,memory-backend=mem \
  -cpu andes-ax45mpv,vlen=512 \
  -nographic \
  -semihosting-config enable=on,target=native \
  -bios "$VSIM_ELF" \
  -plugin "$VSIM_PLUGIN,$PLUGIN_ARGS" >/dev/null 2>&1

if [ ! -s "$STATE_QEMU" ]; then
  echo "FAIL[$FIXTURE]: QEMU 1 did not produce state"
  exit 1
fi

echo "[$FIXTURE 2/3 mode=$TRIGGER_MODE] vsim (shared mmap): resume + drain + spawn QEMU 2"
set +e
timeout "$TIMEOUT" "$VSIM_BIN" "$VSIM_ELF" \
  --resume-from-qemu "$STATE_QEMU" \
  --handoff-out "$STATE_VSIM" \
  --shared-mem-path "$SHARED_MEM" \
  --shared-mem-size "$SHARED_SIZE" \
  --shared-mem-base 0x00000000 \
  --qemu-binary "$VSIM_QEMU" \
  "${VSIM_TRIG_ARGS[@]}" >"$SIM_LOG" 2>&1
RC=$?
set -e

if [ "$RC" != "$EXPECT_RC" ]; then
  echo "FAIL[$FIXTURE]: expected exit $EXPECT_RC, got $RC"
  echo "--- sim log ---"
  cat "$SIM_LOG"
  exit 1
fi

echo "[$FIXTURE 3/3 mode=$TRIGGER_MODE] PASS: round-trip exit code $RC"

1	#!/usr/bin/env bash
2	# End-to-end roundtrip:
3	#   1. QEMU 1 + plugin: drains state on entry trigger.
4	#   2. vsim: resumes, runs body, drains on exit trigger, exits 200.
5	#   3. vsim's --qemu-binary path: spawns QEMU 2 with -gdb -S -semihosting,
6	#      restores GPRs+PC via gdbstub, continues.
7	#   4. QEMU 2 hits the RISC-V semihosting ebreak-triple, exits with the
8	#      semihosting exit_code (low 8 bits of a2 in the existing fixture).
9	#
10	# Two trigger modes (TRIGGER_MODE env, default csr):
11	#   csr  - QEMU plugin pattern-matches `csrwi 0x7C0, 0` to drain on entry;
12	#          vsim pre-scans the ELF and drains on `csrwi 0x7C0, 1` retire.
13	#   pc   - orchestrator resolves _hybrid_enter_pc / _hybrid_exit_pc from the
14	#          ELF symbol table, passes enter_pc=0x... to the plugin and
15	#          --hybrid-exit-pc=0x... to vsim. The fixture's csrwi insns are
16	#          still in the binary (CSR 0x7C0 is a benign vendor CSR write on
17	#          andes-ax45mp + the AX45MPV RTL) but the matchers are bypassed.
18	#
19	# Fixture-parameterized:
20	#   FIXTURE       name of .elf under hybrid/test/  (default: handoff_roundtrip)
21	#   EXPECT_RC     expected vsim process exit code  (default: 3)
22	#   TRIGGER_MODE  csr | pc                          (default: csr)
23	#
24	# Required environment:
25	#   VSIM_BIN         path to sim_<cpu> binary (host-runnable)
26	#   VSIM_QEMU        path to qemu-system-riscv64
27	#   VSIM_PLUGIN      path to libhybrid_handoff.so
28	#   VSIM_ELF         path to <fixture>.elf  (overrides FIXTURE if set)
29	#   NM               riscv64-unknown-elf-nm  (default auto-detect)
30	
31	set -euo pipefail
32	
33	SCRIPT_DIR=$(cd -- "$(dirname -- "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)
34	REPO_ROOT=$(cd -- "$SCRIPT_DIR/../.." && pwd)
35	
36	FIXTURE="${FIXTURE:-handoff_roundtrip}"
37	EXPECT_RC="${EXPECT_RC:-3}"
38	TRIGGER_MODE="${TRIGGER_MODE:-csr}"
39	
40	VSIM_BIN="${VSIM_BIN:-$REPO_ROOT/build/sim_ax45mpv_premium}"
41	VSIM_QEMU="${VSIM_QEMU:-/local/nick/qemu_v5/build/qemu-system-riscv64}"
42	VSIM_PLUGIN="${VSIM_PLUGIN:-$REPO_ROOT/hybrid/qemu_plugin/libhybrid_handoff.so}"
43	VSIM_ELF="${VSIM_ELF:-$REPO_ROOT/hybrid/test/${FIXTURE}.elf}"
44	NM="${NM:-/local/nick/SW_Release/build-ast540/build-toolchain/linux/nds64le-elf-newlib-v5d/bin/riscv64-unknown-elf-nm}"
45	
46	for f in "$VSIM_BIN" "$VSIM_QEMU" "$VSIM_PLUGIN" "$VSIM_ELF"; do
47	  if [ ! -e "$f" ]; then
48	    echo "skip: $f not built; run 'make' under hybrid/test and hybrid/qemu_plugin"
49	    exit 77   # CTest skip code when SKIP_RETURN_CODE is set
50	  fi
51	done
52	
53	case "$TRIGGER_MODE" in
54	  csr) ;;
55	  pc)
56	    if ! command -v "$NM" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
57	      echo "skip[$FIXTURE pc]: nm not found at $NM"
58	      exit 77
59	    fi
60	    # Capture nm output once: avoids racing awk's `exit` against nm's pipe
61	    # writes (large C-fixture symbol tables make nm SIGPIPE under pipefail).
62	    NM_OUT=$("$NM" --defined-only "$VSIM_ELF")
63	    ENTER_PC=$(printf '%s\n' "$NM_OUT" \
64	               | awk '$3=="_hybrid_enter_pc"{print $1}')
65	    EXIT_PC=$(printf '%s\n' "$NM_OUT" \
66	              | awk '$3=="_hybrid_exit_pc"{print $1}')
67	    if [ -z "${ENTER_PC:-}" ] || [ -z "${EXIT_PC:-}" ]; then
68	      echo "FAIL[$FIXTURE pc]: missing _hybrid_enter_pc/_hybrid_exit_pc symbols"
69	      exit 1
70	    fi
71	    ;;
72	  *)
73	    echo "FAIL[$FIXTURE]: unknown TRIGGER_MODE=$TRIGGER_MODE (use csr|pc)"
74	    exit 1
75	    ;;
76	esac
77	
78	STATE_QEMU=$(mktemp)
79	STATE_VSIM=$(mktemp)
80	SIM_LOG=$(mktemp)
81	trap 'rm -f "$STATE_QEMU" "$STATE_VSIM" "$SIM_LOG"' EXIT
82	
83	PLUGIN_ARGS="outfile=$STATE_QEMU"
84	VSIM_TRIG_ARGS=()
85	if [ "$TRIGGER_MODE" = "pc" ]; then
86	  PLUGIN_ARGS="$PLUGIN_ARGS,enter_pc=0x$ENTER_PC"
87	  VSIM_TRIG_ARGS=(--hybrid-exit-pc "0x$EXIT_PC")
88	fi
89	
90	echo "[$FIXTURE 1/3 mode=$TRIGGER_MODE] QEMU 1: drain entry state -> $STATE_QEMU"
91	# -cpu andes-ax45mpv,vlen=512:
92	#   - andes-ax45mpv gates Andes-specific CSRs (mcache_ctl, mmisc_ctl,
93	#     mxstatus, uitb, jvt, mdcause, ...) that the vsim-demo crt0/handler
94	#     touch; default rv64 illegal-traps on the first mcache_ctl write.
95	#   - vlen=512 pins QEMU's VLEN to 512 bits (vlenb=64), matching the vsim
96	#     RTL build (data/configs/ax45mpv_premium.cfg: NDS_VLEN 512) and
97	#     HYBRID_VLEN_BYTES_MAX=64 in include/hybrid/state_abi.h. The default
98	#     andes-ax45mpv has VLEN=1024 which silently truncates V-state in
99	#     the wire format. Non-V fixtures run identically under either VLEN.
100	# -semihosting-config enable=on,target=native: lets fixtures print from
101	# QEMU1 (phase 1) via newlib stdio (linked against libgloss_vh), matching
102	# the QEMU2 launch in qemu_handback.hpp. Phase 2 (vsim) is forbidden from
103	# doing any syscall -- vsim does not implement semihosting.
104	"$VSIM_QEMU" -M virt -cpu andes-ax45mpv,vlen=512 -bios none -nographic \
105	  -semihosting-config enable=on,target=native \
106	  -kernel "$VSIM_ELF" \
107	  -plugin "$VSIM_PLUGIN,$PLUGIN_ARGS" >/dev/null 2>&1
108	
109	if [ ! -s "$STATE_QEMU" ]; then
110	  echo "FAIL[$FIXTURE]: QEMU 1 did not produce state"
111	  exit 1
112	fi
113	
114	echo "[$FIXTURE 2/3 mode=$TRIGGER_MODE] vsim: resume + drain + spawn QEMU 2"
115	set +e
116	timeout 60 "$VSIM_BIN" "$VSIM_ELF" \
117	  --resume-from-qemu "$STATE_QEMU" \
118	  --handoff-out "$STATE_VSIM" \
119	  --qemu-binary "$VSIM_QEMU" \
120	  "${VSIM_TRIG_ARGS[@]}" >"$SIM_LOG" 2>&1
121	RC=$?
122	set -e
123	
124	if [ "$RC" != "$EXPECT_RC" ]; then
125	  echo "FAIL[$FIXTURE]: expected exit $EXPECT_RC, got $RC"
126	  echo "--- sim log ---"
127	  cat "$SIM_LOG"
128	  exit 1
129	fi
130	
131	echo "[$FIXTURE 3/3 mode=$TRIGGER_MODE] PASS: round-trip exit code $RC"
132

STATE_QEMU=$(mktemp)
STATE_VSIM=$(mktemp)
SIM_LOG=$(mktemp)
trap 'rm -f "$STATE_QEMU" "$STATE_VSIM" "$SIM_LOG"' EXIT

PLUGIN_ARGS="outfile=$STATE_QEMU"
VSIM_TRIG_ARGS=()
if [ "$TRIGGER_MODE" = "pc" ]; then
  PLUGIN_ARGS="$PLUGIN_ARGS,enter_pc=0x$ENTER_PC"
  VSIM_TRIG_ARGS=(--hybrid-exit-pc "0x$EXIT_PC")
fi

echo "[$FIXTURE 1/3 mode=$TRIGGER_MODE] QEMU 1: drain entry state -> $STATE_QEMU"
# -cpu andes-ax45mpv,vlen=512:
#   - andes-ax45mpv gates Andes-specific CSRs (mcache_ctl, mmisc_ctl,
#     mxstatus, uitb, jvt, mdcause, ...) that the vsim-demo crt0/handler
#     touch; default rv64 illegal-traps on the first mcache_ctl write.
#   - vlen=512 pins QEMU's VLEN to 512 bits (vlenb=64), matching the vsim
#     RTL build (data/configs/ax45mpv_premium.cfg: NDS_VLEN 512) and
#     HYBRID_VLEN_BYTES_MAX=64 in include/hybrid/state_abi.h. The default
#     andes-ax45mpv has VLEN=1024 which silently truncates V-state in
#     the wire format. Non-V fixtures run identically under either VLEN.
# -semihosting-config enable=on,target=native: lets fixtures print from
# QEMU1 (phase 1) via newlib stdio (linked against libgloss_vh), matching
# the QEMU2 launch in qemu_handback.hpp. Phase 2 (vsim) is forbidden from
# doing any syscall -- vsim does not implement semihosting.
"$VSIM_QEMU" -M virt -cpu andes-ax45mpv,vlen=512 -bios none -nographic \
  -semihosting-config enable=on,target=native \
  -kernel "$VSIM_ELF" \
  -plugin "$VSIM_PLUGIN,$PLUGIN_ARGS" >/dev/null 2>&1

if [ ! -s "$STATE_QEMU" ]; then
  echo "FAIL[$FIXTURE]: QEMU 1 did not produce state"
  exit 1
fi

echo "[$FIXTURE 2/3 mode=$TRIGGER_MODE] vsim: resume + drain + spawn QEMU 2"
set +e
timeout 60 "$VSIM_BIN" "$VSIM_ELF" \
  --resume-from-qemu "$STATE_QEMU" \
  --handoff-out "$STATE_VSIM" \
  --qemu-binary "$VSIM_QEMU" \
  "${VSIM_TRIG_ARGS[@]}" >"$SIM_LOG" 2>&1
RC=$?
set -e

if [ "$RC" != "$EXPECT_RC" ]; then
  echo "FAIL[$FIXTURE]: expected exit $EXPECT_RC, got $RC"
  echo "--- sim log ---"
  cat "$SIM_LOG"
  exit 1
fi

echo "[$FIXTURE 3/3 mode=$TRIGGER_MODE] PASS: round-trip exit code $RC"

108	if(VSIM_ENABLE_HYBRID)

110	    message(WARNING
111	      "VSIM_ENABLE_HYBRID=ON but qemu-plugin.h not found under "
112	      "${VSIM_QEMU_ROOT}/include/qemu/. Build the TCG plugin out-of-tree via "
113	      "hybrid_sim/qemu_plugin/Makefile on a host that has QEMU installed.")
114	  endif()
115	  message(STATUS "Hybrid mode: enabled (QEMU root: ${VSIM_QEMU_ROOT})")
116	
117	  # Round-trip e2e tests. Each entry registers one CTest case using the
118	  # parameterized roundtrip_e2e.sh harness. The harness skips (return 77)
119	  # when QEMU/plugin/ELF artifacts are missing, so the same CTestTestfile
120	  # works whether ctest is invoked from the container's /work/build or
121	  # from the host's build/.
122	  set(VSIM_RT_FIXTURES
123	    "handoff_roundtrip:3"
124	    "rt_pc_jump:0"
125	    "rt_sign_bits:0"
126	    "rt_abi_aliases:0"
127	    "rt_long_roi:0"
128	    "rt_all_gprs:0"
129	    "rt_mem_loadstore:0"
130	    "rt_unaligned_lh:0"
131	    "rt_csr_survival:0"
132	  )
133	  foreach(entry ${VSIM_RT_FIXTURES})
134	    string(REPLACE ":" ";" parts ${entry})
135	    list(GET parts 0 fname)
136	    list(GET parts 1 erc)
137	    # CSR-trigger variant (existing path, default mode).
138	    add_test(
139	      NAME test_e2e_${fname}
140	      COMMAND bash -c
141	        "TRIGGER_MODE=csr FIXTURE=${fname} EXPECT_RC=${erc} bash ../tests/hybrid/roundtrip_e2e.sh"
142	    )
143	    # COST: ctest -j greedily schedules highest-cost first to keep the
144	    # tail short. Tier-1 fixtures uniformly run ~15-20s on the CI host.
145	    set_tests_properties(test_e2e_${fname} PROPERTIES
146	      SKIP_RETURN_CODE 77
147	      LABELS "hybrid-e2e;hybrid-e2e-csr"
148	      COST 30
149	    )
150	    # PC-trigger variant: same fixture, _hybrid_{enter,exit}_pc resolved
151	    # from the ELF symbol table. The csrwi insns in the binary are inert
152	    # in this mode (matchers bypassed; CSR 0x7C0 is a benign vendor write
153	    # on andes-ax45mp + AX45MPV).
154	    add_test(
155	      NAME test_e2e_${fname}_pc
156	      COMMAND bash -c
157	        "TRIGGER_MODE=pc FIXTURE=${fname} EXPECT_RC=${erc} bash ../tests/hybrid/roundtrip_e2e.sh"
158	    )
159	    set_tests_properties(test_e2e_${fname}_pc PROPERTIES
160	      SKIP_RETURN_CODE 77
161	      LABELS "hybrid-e2e;hybrid-e2e-pc"
162	      COST 30
163	    )
164	  endforeach()
165	
166	  # Tier-2: separate driver because it allocates /dev/shm and uses
167	  # --shared-mem-path / -object memory-backend-file to share RAM across
168	  # QEMU 1, vsim, and QEMU 2.
169	  # Format: fname:expected_rc[:timeout_s]. Default timeout is 60 (set in
170	  # rt_shared_mem.sh). rt_c_matmul needs more headroom because the GEMM
171	  # kernel runs cycle-accurately under vsim (~45-50s) before drain.
172	  set(VSIM_RT_T2_FIXTURES
173	    "rt_shared_mem:0"
174	    # C-level fixtures: linked against vsim-demo C runtime (crt0/trap/libgloss/
175	    # handler). They need Tier-2 (shared mmap) so the stack and heap survive
176	    # the vsim->QEMU2 boundary -- main()'s ra-spill/restore around the
177	    # csrwi 0x7C0,1 marker would otherwise read uninitialized RAM in QEMU2.
178	    "rt_c_hello:0"
179	    "rt_c_printf:0"
180	    "rt_c_matmul:0:120"
181	    # Regression for the "no redundant ELF reload" rule: .bss survival
182	    # across QEMU1 -> vsim -> QEMU2 without users hand-allocating fixed
183	    # addresses. Fails today because vsim's elf_loader and QEMU 2's
184	    # -kernel both zero-fill the .bss tail of every PT_LOAD.
185	    "rt_c_static_buf:0"
186	    # 1D 3-tap FIR convolution as a real RVV use case spanning the
187	    # handoff: QEMU1 generates x via intrinsics; vsim applies the kernel
188	    # (vslide1up / vslide1down + vadd) using x carried in its compiler-
189	    # picked V register; QEMU2 stores y and scalar-verifies. V regfile
190	    # transport rides on the save-all / reload-all helpers in
191	    # runtime/v_regfile_xfer.{h,c} so the fixture body is pure intrinsics
192	    # with no physical V register references.
193	    "rt_c_v_regs:0"
194	    # V scalar CSR survival: sets vxsat/vxrm/vtype/vl/vstart in vsim phase,
195	    # verifies in QEMU2. Exercises the full Tier-A+B restore path.
196	    "rt_c_v_csrs:0"
197	    # 16x16x16 RVV GEMM under vsim, scalar reference recompute under QEMU 2.
198	    # Same cycle-accurate budget headroom as rt_c_matmul (the V kernel runs
199	    # fewer instructions but issue/execute is implementation-dependent).
200	    "rt_c_v_matmul:0:120"
201	  )
202	  foreach(entry ${VSIM_RT_T2_FIXTURES})
203	    string(REPLACE ":" ";" parts ${entry})
204	    list(GET parts 0 fname)
205	    list(GET parts 1 erc)
206	    list(LENGTH parts nparts)
207	    set(timeout_env "")
208	    set(tcost 60)
209	    if(nparts GREATER 2)
210	      list(GET parts 2 tsec)
211	      set(timeout_env "TIMEOUT=${tsec} ")
212	      # Use the per-fixture timeout as the scheduling cost so long
213	      # fixtures (rt_c_matmul: 120s) start in the first wave under -j.
214	      set(tcost ${tsec})
215	    endif()
216	    # CSR variant.
217	    add_test(
218	      NAME test_e2e_${fname}
219	      COMMAND bash -c
220	        "${timeout_env}TRIGGER_MODE=csr FIXTURE=${fname} EXPECT_RC=${erc} bash ../tests/hybrid/rt_shared_mem.sh"
221	    )
222	    set_tests_properties(test_e2e_${fname} PROPERTIES
223	      SKIP_RETURN_CODE 77
224	      LABELS "hybrid-e2e;hybrid-e2e-csr"
225	      COST ${tcost}
226	    )
227	    # PC variant: same fixture, same timeout, mode flipped.
228	    add_test(
229	      NAME test_e2e_${fname}_pc
230	      COMMAND bash -c
231	        "${timeout_env}TRIGGER_MODE=pc FIXTURE=${fname} EXPECT_RC=${erc} bash ../tests/hybrid/rt_shared_mem.sh"
232	    )
233	    set_tests_properties(test_e2e_${fname}_pc PROPERTIES
234	      SKIP_RETURN_CODE 77
235	      LABELS "hybrid-e2e;hybrid-e2e-pc"
236	      COST ${tcost}
237	    )
238	  endforeach()
239	
240	  # Negative / robustness drivers: each guards a quietly-degrading failure
241	  # path the happy-path round-trips can't see (corrupted state file,
242	  # missing --qemu-binary, silent gdbstub peer, tty-induced SIGTTOU).
243	  # No PC variants - these assert orchestrator failure modes that are
244	  # orthogonal to the trigger mechanism.
245	  foreach(neg corrupt_state no_qemu_binary gdbstub_timeout pty_handback)
246	    add_test(NAME test_e2e_rt_${neg}
247	      COMMAND bash ../tests/hybrid/rt_${neg}.sh)
248	    set_tests_properties(test_e2e_rt_${neg} PROPERTIES
249	      SKIP_RETURN_CODE 77

file.content:
add_dependencies(check test_handoff_controller)

vsim_add_test(test_v_regfile_xfer tests/hybrid/v_regfile_xfer.test.cpp)
target_include_directories(test_v_regfile_xfer PRIVATE
  "${CMAKE_SOURCE_DIR}/../include"
)
add_dependencies(check test_v_regfile_xfer)

if(VSIM_ENABLE_HYBRID)
  if(NOT EXISTS "${VSIM_QEMU_ROOT}/include/qemu/qemu-plugin.h")
    message(WARNING
      "VSIM_ENABLE_HYBRID=ON but qemu-plugin.h not found under "
      "${VSIM_QEMU_ROOT}/include/qemu/. Build the TCG plugin out-of-tree via "
      "hybrid_sim/qemu_plugin/Makefile on a host that has QEMU installed.")
  endif()
  message(STATUS "Hybrid mode: enabled (QEMU root: ${VSIM_QEMU_ROOT})")

  # Round-trip e2e tests. Each entry registers one CTest case using the
  # parameterized roundtrip_e2e.sh harness. The harness skips (return 77)
  # when QEMU/plugin/ELF artifacts are missing, so the same CTestTestfile
  # works whether ctest is invoked from the container's /work/build or
  # from the host's build/.
  set(VSIM_RT_FIXTURES
    "handoff_roundtrip:3"
    "rt_pc_jump:0"
    "rt_sign_bits:0"
    "rt_abi_aliases:0"
    "rt_long_roi:0"
    "rt_all_gprs:0"
    "rt_mem_loadstore:0"
    "rt_unaligned_lh:0"
    "rt_csr_survival:0"
  )
  foreach(entry ${VSIM_RT_FIXTURES})
    string(REPLACE ":" ";" parts ${entry})
    list(GET parts 0 fname)
    list(GET parts 1 erc)
    # CSR-trigger variant (existing path, default mode).
    add_test(
      NAME test_e2e_${fname}
      COMMAND bash -c
        "TRIGGER_MODE=csr FIXTURE=${fname} EXPECT_RC=${erc} bash ../tests/hybrid/roundtrip_e2e.sh"
    )
    # COST: ctest -j greedily schedules highest-cost first to keep the
    # tail short. Tier-1 fixtures uniformly run ~15-20s on the CI host.
    set_tests_properties(test_e2e_${fname} PROPERTIES
      SKIP_RETURN_CODE 77
      LABELS "hybrid-e2e;hybrid-e2e-csr"
      COST 30
    )
    # PC-trigger variant: same fixture, _hybrid_{enter,exit}_pc resolved
    # from the ELF symbol table. The csrwi insns in the binary are inert
    # in this mode (matchers bypassed; CSR 0x7C0 is a benign vendor write
    # on andes-ax45mp + AX45MPV).
    add_test(
      NAME test_e2e_${fname}_pc
      COMMAND bash -c
        "TRIGGER_MODE=pc FIXTURE=${fname} EXPECT_RC=${erc} bash ../tests/hybrid/roundtrip_e2e.sh"
    )
    set_tests_properties(test_e2e_${fname}_pc PROPERTIES
      SKIP_RETURN_CODE 77
      LABELS "hybrid-e2e;hybrid-e2e-pc"
      COST 30
    )
  endforeach()

  # Tier-2: separate driver because it allocates /dev/shm and uses
  # --shared-mem-path / -object memory-backend-file to share RAM across
  # QEMU 1, vsim, and QEMU 2.
  # Format: fname:expected_rc[:timeout_s]. Default timeout is 60 (set in
  # rt_shared_mem.sh). rt_c_matmul needs more headroom because the GEMM
  # kernel runs cycle-accurately under vsim (~45-50s) before drain.
  set(VSIM_RT_T2_FIXTURES
    "rt_shared_mem:0"
    # C-level fixtures: linked against vsim-demo C runtime (crt0/trap/libgloss/
    # handler). They need Tier-2 (shared mmap) so the stack and heap survive
    # the vsim->QEMU2 boundary -- main()'s ra-spill/restore around the
    # csrwi 0x7C0,1 marker would otherwise read uninitialized RAM in QEMU2.
    "rt_c_hello:0"
    "rt_c_printf:0"
    "rt_c_matmul:0:120"
    # Regression for the "no redundant ELF reload" rule: .bss survival
    # across QEMU1 -> vsim -> QEMU2 without users hand-allocating fixed
    # addresses. Fails today because vsim's elf_loader and QEMU 2's
    # -kernel both zero-fill the .bss tail of every PT_LOAD.
    "rt_c_static_buf:0"
    # 1D 3-tap FIR convolution as a real RVV use case spanning the
    # handoff: QEMU1 generates x via intrinsics; vsim applies the kernel
    # (vslide1up / vslide1down + vadd) using x carried in its compiler-
    # picked V register; QEMU2 stores y and scalar-verifies. V regfile
    # transport rides on the save-all / reload-all helpers in
    # runtime/v_regfile_xfer.{h,c} so the fixture body is pure intrinsics
    # with no physical V register references.
    "rt_c_v_regs:0"
    # V scalar CSR survival: sets vxsat/vxrm/vtype/vl/vstart in vsim phase,
    # verifies in QEMU2. Exercises the full Tier-A+B restore path.
    "rt_c_v_csrs:0"
    # 16x16x16 RVV GEMM under vsim, scalar reference recompute under QEMU 2.
    # Same cycle-accurate budget headroom as rt_c_matmul (the V kernel runs
    # fewer instructions but issue/execute is implementation-dependent).
    "rt_c_v_matmul:0:120"
  )
  foreach(entry ${VSIM_RT_T2_FIXTURES})
    string(REPLACE ":" ";" parts ${entry})
    list(GET parts 0 fname)
    list(GET parts 1 erc)
    list(LENGTH parts nparts)
    set(timeout_env "")
    set(tcost 60)
    if(nparts GREATER 2)
      list(GET parts 2 tsec)
      set(timeout_env "TIMEOUT=${tsec} ")
      # Use the per-fixture timeout as the scheduling cost so long
      # fixtures (rt_c_matmul: 120s) start in the first wave under -j.
      set(tcost ${tsec})
    endif()
    # CSR variant.
    add_test(
      NAME test_e2e_${fname}
      COMMAND bash -c
        "${timeout_env}TRIGGER_MODE=csr FIXTURE=${fname} EXPECT_RC=${erc} bash ../tests/hybrid/rt_shared_mem.sh"
    )
    set_tests_properties(test_e2e_${fname} PROPERTIES
      SKIP_RETURN_CODE 77
      LABELS "hybrid-e2e;hybrid-e2e-csr"
      COST ${tcost}
    )
    # PC variant: same fixture, same timeout, mode flipped.
    add_test(
      NAME test_e2e_${fname}_pc
      COMMAND bash -c
        "${timeout_env}TRIGGER_MODE=pc FIXTURE=${fname} EXPECT_RC=${erc} bash ../tests/hybrid/rt_shared_mem.sh"
    )
    set_tests_properties(test_e2e_${fname}_pc PROPERTIES
      SKIP_RETURN_CODE 77
      LABELS "hybrid-e2e;hybrid-e2e-pc"
      COST ${tcost}
    )
  endforeach()

  # Negative / robustness drivers: each guards a quietly-degrading failure
  # path the happy-path round-trips can't see (corrupted state file,
  # missing --qemu-binary, silent gdbstub peer, tty-induced SIGTTOU).
  # No PC variants - these assert orchestrator failure modes that are
  # orthogonal to the trigger mechanism.
  foreach(neg corrupt_state no_qemu_binary gdbstub_timeout pty_handback)
    add_test(NAME test_e2e_rt_${neg}
      COMMAND bash ../tests/hybrid/rt_${neg}.sh)
    set_tests_properties(test_e2e_rt_${neg} PROPERTIES
      SKIP_RETURN_CODE 77

file.content:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Negative test: --qemu-binary points at a missing file. Vsim's drain must
# still fire and the spawn-fail must propagate as exit 127 (the standard
# exec-failure code), not as a 5 s connect_loopback timeout that masquerades
# as something else, and not as a hang that ties up CTest.

set -euo pipefail

SCRIPT_DIR=$(cd -- "$(dirname -- "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)
REPO_ROOT=$(cd -- "$SCRIPT_DIR/../.." && pwd)

VSIM_BIN="${VSIM_BIN:-$REPO_ROOT/build/sim_ax45mpv_premium}"
VSIM_QEMU="${VSIM_QEMU:-/local/nick/qemu_v5/build/qemu-system-riscv64}"
VSIM_PLUGIN="${VSIM_PLUGIN:-$REPO_ROOT/hybrid/qemu_plugin/libhybrid_handoff.so}"
VSIM_ELF="${VSIM_ELF:-$REPO_ROOT/hybrid/test/handoff_roundtrip.elf}"

for f in "$VSIM_BIN" "$VSIM_QEMU" "$VSIM_PLUGIN" "$VSIM_ELF"; do
  if [ ! -e "$f" ]; then
    echo "skip: $f not built"
    exit 77
  fi
done

STATE_QEMU=$(mktemp)
STATE_VSIM=$(mktemp)
SIM_LOG=$(mktemp)
trap 'rm -f "$STATE_QEMU" "$STATE_VSIM" "$SIM_LOG"' EXIT

# Phase A: real QEMU 1 produces a valid entry-state snapshot so vsim has
# something to resume from and will reach the exit-csrwi drain.
"$VSIM_QEMU" -M virt -bios none -nographic \
  -kernel "$VSIM_ELF" \
  -plugin "$VSIM_PLUGIN,outfile=$STATE_QEMU" >/dev/null 2>&1

if [ ! -s "$STATE_QEMU" ]; then
  echo "FAIL[rt_no_qemu_binary]: QEMU 1 did not produce state"
  exit 1
fi

START=$(date +%s)
set +e
timeout 30 "$VSIM_BIN" "$VSIM_ELF" \
  --resume-from-qemu "$STATE_QEMU" \
  --handoff-out "$STATE_VSIM" \
  --qemu-binary /nonexistent/qemu-system-riscv64 >"$SIM_LOG" 2>&1
RC=$?
set -e
ELAPSED=$(($(date +%s) - START))

if [ "$RC" -ne 127 ]; then
  echo "FAIL[rt_no_qemu_binary]: expected exit 127 (exec failure), got $RC"
  echo "elapsed=${ELAPSED}s"
  echo "--- sim log ---"
  cat "$SIM_LOG"
  exit 1
fi

if [ "$ELAPSED" -gt 15 ]; then
  echo "FAIL[rt_no_qemu_binary]: vsim took ${ELAPSED}s (>15s suggests a hang)"
  exit 1
fi

# No zombie left: any leftover child of this shell would show up here.
if jobs -r | grep -q .; then
  echo "FAIL[rt_no_qemu_binary]: background jobs still running"
  exit 1
fi

echo "[rt_no_qemu_binary] PASS: vsim exit=$RC elapsed=${ELAPSED}s"

#!/usr/bin/env bash
# F1 contract: QEMU plugin exits with code 200 on drain (matches vsim's
# kExitHandoffToQemu). 0 is reserved for "workload completed normally".
# The orchestrator (F2+) uses this distinction to decide the next spawn.
#
# Required environment (auto-detected with sensible defaults):
#   VSIM_QEMU    qemu-system-riscv64
#   VSIM_PLUGIN  libhybrid_handoff.so
#   VSIM_ELF     a CSR-trigger fixture (default: handoff_roundtrip.elf)

set -euo pipefail

SCRIPT_DIR=$(cd -- "$(dirname -- "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)
REPO_ROOT=$(cd -- "$SCRIPT_DIR/../.." && pwd)

VSIM_QEMU="${VSIM_QEMU:-/local/nick/qemu_v5/build/qemu-system-riscv64}"
VSIM_PLUGIN="${VSIM_PLUGIN:-$REPO_ROOT/hybrid/qemu_plugin/libhybrid_handoff.so}"
VSIM_ELF="${VSIM_ELF:-$REPO_ROOT/hybrid/test/handoff_roundtrip.elf}"

for f in "$VSIM_QEMU" "$VSIM_PLUGIN" "$VSIM_ELF"; do
  if [ ! -e "$f" ]; then
    echo "skip: $f not built"
    exit 77
  fi
done

STATE_QEMU=$(mktemp)
QEMU_LOG=$(mktemp)
trap 'rm -f "$STATE_QEMU" "$QEMU_LOG"' EXIT

set +e
"$VSIM_QEMU" -M virt -cpu andes-ax45mpv,vlen=512 -bios none -nographic \
  -semihosting-config enable=on,target=native \
  -kernel "$VSIM_ELF" \
  -plugin "$VSIM_PLUGIN,outfile=$STATE_QEMU" >"$QEMU_LOG" 2>&1
RC=$?
set -e

if [ "$RC" -ne 200 ]; then
  echo "FAIL[plugin_drain_exit_code]: expected QEMU exit=200 on drain, got $RC"
  echo "--- qemu log ---"
  cat "$QEMU_LOG"
  exit 1
fi

if [ ! -s "$STATE_QEMU" ]; then
  echo "FAIL[plugin_drain_exit_code]: state file is empty"
  exit 1
fi

echo "[plugin_drain_exit_code] PASS: QEMU exit=$RC, state size=$(wc -c <"$STATE_QEMU") bytes"

  CACHE PATH "Path to QEMU source tree used to build the hybrid TCG plugin")

# The ABI test is always built so struct layout drift is caught even in
# non-hybrid builds. It has no SystemC/Verilator dependency.
vsim_add_test(test_state_abi tests/hybrid/state_abi.test.cpp)
target_include_directories(test_state_abi PRIVATE
  "${CMAKE_SOURCE_DIR}/../include"
)
add_dependencies(check test_state_abi)

  "${CMAKE_SOURCE_DIR}/../include"
)
add_dependencies(check test_v_regfile_xfer)

if(VSIM_ENABLE_HYBRID)
  if(NOT EXISTS "${VSIM_QEMU_ROOT}/include/qemu/qemu-plugin.h")
    message(WARNING
      "VSIM_ENABLE_HYBRID=ON but qemu-plugin.h not found under "
      "${VSIM_QEMU_ROOT}/include/qemu/. Build the TCG plugin out-of-tree via "
      "hybrid_sim/qemu_plugin/Makefile on a host that has QEMU installed.")
  endif()
  message(STATUS "Hybrid mode: enabled (QEMU root: ${VSIM_QEMU_ROOT})")

  # Round-trip e2e tests. Each entry registers one CTest case using the
  # parameterized roundtrip_e2e.sh harness. The harness skips (return 77)
  # when QEMU/plugin/ELF artifacts are missing, so the same CTestTestfile
  # works whether ctest is invoked from the container's /work/build or
  # from the host's build/.
  set(VSIM_RT_FIXTURES
    "handoff_roundtrip:3"
    "rt_pc_jump:0"
    "rt_sign_bits:0"
    "rt_abi_aliases:0"
    "rt_long_roi:0"
    "rt_all_gprs:0"
    "rt_mem_loadstore:0"
    "rt_unaligned_lh:0"
    "rt_csr_survival:0"
  )
  foreach(entry ${VSIM_RT_FIXTURES})
    string(REPLACE ":" ";" parts ${entry})
    list(GET parts 0 fname)
    list(GET parts 1 erc)
    # CSR-trigger variant (existing path, default mode).
    add_test(
      NAME test_e2e_${fname}
      COMMAND bash -c
        "TRIGGER_MODE=csr FIXTURE=${fname} EXPECT_RC=${erc} bash ../tests/hybrid/roundtrip_e2e.sh"
    )
    # COST: ctest -j greedily schedules highest-cost first to keep the
    # tail short. Tier-1 fixtures uniformly run ~15-20s on the CI host.
    set_tests_properties(test_e2e_${fname} PROPERTIES
      SKIP_RETURN_CODE 77
      LABELS "hybrid-e2e;hybrid-e2e-csr"
      COST 30
    )
    # PC-trigger variant: same fixture, _hybrid_{enter,exit}_pc resolved
    # from the ELF symbol table. The csrwi insns in the binary are inert
    # in this mode (matchers bypassed; CSR 0x7C0 is a benign vendor write
    # on andes-ax45mp + AX45MPV).
    add_test(
      NAME test_e2e_${fname}_pc
      COMMAND bash -c
        "TRIGGER_MODE=pc FIXTURE=${fname} EXPECT_RC=${erc} bash ../tests/hybrid/roundtrip_e2e.sh"
    )
    set_tests_properties(test_e2e_${fname}_pc PROPERTIES
      SKIP_RETURN_CODE 77
      LABELS "hybrid-e2e;hybrid-e2e-pc"
      COST 30
    )
  endforeach()

  # Tier-2: separate driver because it allocates /dev/shm and uses
  # --shared-mem-path / -object memory-backend-file to share RAM across
  # QEMU 1, vsim, and QEMU 2.
  # Format: fname:expected_rc[:timeout_s]. Default timeout is 60 (set in
  # rt_shared_mem.sh). rt_c_matmul needs more headroom because the GEMM
  # kernel runs cycle-accurately under vsim (~45-50s) before drain.
  set(VSIM_RT_T2_FIXTURES
    "rt_shared_mem:0"
    # C-level fixtures: linked against vsim-demo C runtime (crt0/trap/libgloss/
    # handler). They need Tier-2 (shared mmap) so the stack and heap survive
    # the vsim->QEMU2 boundary -- main()'s ra-spill/restore around the
    # csrwi 0x7C0,1 marker would otherwise read uninitialized RAM in QEMU2.
    "rt_c_hello:0"
    "rt_c_printf:0"
    "rt_c_matmul:0:120"
    # Regression for the "no redundant ELF reload" rule: .bss survival
    # across QEMU1 -> vsim -> QEMU2 without users hand-allocating fixed
    # addresses. Fails today because vsim's elf_loader and QEMU 2's
    # -kernel both zero-fill the .bss tail of every PT_LOAD.
    "rt_c_static_buf:0"
    # 1D 3-tap FIR convolution as a real RVV use case spanning the
    # handoff: QEMU1 generates x via intrinsics; vsim applies the kernel
    # (vslide1up / vslide1down + vadd) using x carried in its compiler-
    # picked V register; QEMU2 stores y and scalar-verifies. V regfile
    # transport rides on the save-all / reload-all helpers in
    # runtime/v_regfile_xfer.{h,c} so the fixture body is pure intrinsics
    # with no physical V register references.
    "rt_c_v_regs:0"
    # V scalar CSR survival: sets vxsat/vxrm/vtype/vl/vstart in vsim phase,
    # verifies in QEMU2. Exercises the full Tier-A+B restore path.
    "rt_c_v_csrs:0"
    # 16x16x16 RVV GEMM under vsim, scalar reference recompute under QEMU 2.
    # Same cycle-accurate budget headroom as rt_c_matmul (the V kernel runs
    # fewer instructions but issue/execute is implementation-dependent).
    "rt_c_v_matmul:0:120"
  )
  foreach(entry ${VSIM_RT_T2_FIXTURES})
    string(REPLACE ":" ";" parts ${entry})
    list(GET parts 0 fname)
    list(GET parts 1 erc)
    list(LENGTH parts nparts)
    set(timeout_env "")
    set(tcost 60)
    if(nparts GREATER 2)
      list(GET parts 2 tsec)
      set(timeout_env "TIMEOUT=${tsec} ")
      # Use the per-fixture timeout as the scheduling cost so long
      # fixtures (rt_c_matmul: 120s) start in the first wave under -j.
      set(tcost ${tsec})
    endif()
    # CSR variant.
    add_test(
      NAME test_e2e_${fname}
      COMMAND bash -c
        "${timeout_env}TRIGGER_MODE=csr FIXTURE=${fname} EXPECT_RC=${erc} bash ../tests/hybrid/rt_shared_mem.sh"
    )
    set_tests_properties(test_e2e_${fname} PROPERTIES
      SKIP_RETURN_CODE 77
      LABELS "hybrid-e2e;hybrid-e2e-csr"
      COST ${tcost}
    )
    # PC variant: same fixture, same timeout, mode flipped.
    add_test(
      NAME test_e2e_${fname}_pc
      COMMAND bash -c
        "${timeout_env}TRIGGER_MODE=pc FIXTURE=${fname} EXPECT_RC=${erc} bash ../tests/hybrid/rt_shared_mem.sh"
    )
    set_tests_properties(test_e2e_${fname}_pc PROPERTIES
      SKIP_RETURN_CODE 77
      LABELS "hybrid-e2e;hybrid-e2e-pc"
      COST ${tcost}
    )
  endforeach()

  # Negative / robustness drivers: each guards a quietly-degrading failure
  # path the happy-path round-trips can't see (corrupted state file,
  # missing --qemu-binary, silent gdbstub peer, tty-induced SIGTTOU).
  # No PC variants - these assert orchestrator failure modes that are
  # orthogonal to the trigger mechanism.
  foreach(neg corrupt_state no_qemu_binary gdbstub_timeout pty_handback)
    add_test(NAME test_e2e_rt_${neg}
      COMMAND bash ../tests/hybrid/rt_${neg}.sh)
    set_tests_properties(test_e2e_rt_${neg} PROPERTIES
      SKIP_RETURN_CODE 77
      LABELS "hybrid-e2e"
    )
  endforeach()
endif()

toolUseResult.originalFile:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# End-to-end roundtrip:
#   1. QEMU 1 + plugin: drains state on entry trigger.
#   2. vsim: resumes, runs body, drains on exit trigger, exits 200.
#   3. vsim's --qemu-binary path: spawns QEMU 2 with -gdb -S -semihosting,
#      restores GPRs+PC via gdbstub, continues.
#   4. QEMU 2 hits the RISC-V semihosting ebreak-triple, exits with the
#      semihosting exit_code (low 8 bits of a2 in the existing fixture).
#
# Two trigger modes (TRIGGER_MODE env, default csr):
#   csr  - QEMU plugin pattern-matches `csrwi 0x7C0, 0` to drain on entry;
#          vsim pre-scans the ELF and drains on `csrwi 0x7C0, 1` retire.
#   pc   - orchestrator resolves _hybrid_enter_pc / _hybrid_exit_pc from the
#          ELF symbol table, passes enter_pc=0x... to the plugin and
#          --hybrid-exit-pc=0x... to vsim. The fixture's csrwi insns are
#          still in the binary (CSR 0x7C0 is a benign vendor CSR write on
#          andes-ax45mp + the AX45MPV RTL) but the matchers are bypassed.
#
# Fixture-parameterized:
#   FIXTURE       name of .elf under hybrid/test/  (default: handoff_roundtrip)
#   EXPECT_RC     expected vsim process exit code  (default: 3)
#   TRIGGER_MODE  csr | pc                          (default: csr)
#
# Required environment:
#   VSIM_BIN         path to sim_<cpu> binary (host-runnable)
#   VSIM_QEMU        path to qemu-system-riscv64
#   VSIM_PLUGIN      path to libhybrid_handoff.so
#   VSIM_ELF         path to <fixture>.elf  (overrides FIXTURE if set)
#   NM               riscv64-unknown-elf-nm  (default auto-detect)

set -euo pipefail

SCRIPT_DIR=$(cd -- "$(dirname -- "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)
REPO_ROOT=$(cd -- "$SCRIPT_DIR/../.." && pwd)

FIXTURE="${FIXTURE:-handoff_roundtrip}"
EXPECT_RC="${EXPECT_RC:-3}"
TRIGGER_MODE="${TRIGGER_MODE:-csr}"

VSIM_BIN="${VSIM_BIN:-$REPO_ROOT/build/sim_ax45mpv_premium}"
VSIM_QEMU="${VSIM_QEMU:-/local/nick/qemu_v5/build/qemu-system-riscv64}"
VSIM_PLUGIN="${VSIM_PLUGIN:-$REPO_ROOT/hybrid/qemu_plugin/libhybrid_handoff.so}"
VSIM_ELF="${VSIM_ELF:-$REPO_ROOT/hybrid/test/${FIXTURE}.elf}"
NM="${NM:-/local/nick/SW_Release/build-ast540/build-toolchain/linux/nds64le-elf-newlib-v5d/bin/riscv64-unknown-elf-nm}"

for f in "$VSIM_BIN" "$VSIM_QEMU" "$VSIM_PLUGIN" "$VSIM_ELF"; do
  if [ ! -e "$f" ]; then
    echo "skip: $f not built; run 'make' under hybrid/test and hybrid/qemu_plugin"
    exit 77   # CTest skip code when SKIP_RETURN_CODE is set
  fi
done

case "$TRIGGER_MODE" in
  csr) ;;
  pc)
    if ! command -v "$NM" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
      echo "skip[$FIXTURE pc]: nm not found at $NM"
      exit 77
    fi
    # Capture nm output once: avoids racing awk's `exit` against nm's pipe
    # writes (large C-fixture symbol tables make nm SIGPIPE under pipefail).
    NM_OUT=$("$NM" --defined-only "$VSIM_ELF")
    ENTER_PC=$(printf '%s\n' "$NM_OUT" \
               | awk '$3=="_hybrid_enter_pc"{print $1}')
    EXIT_PC=$(printf '%s\n' "$NM_OUT" \
              | awk '$3=="_hybrid_exit_pc"{print $1}')
    if [ -z "${ENTER_PC:-}" ] || [ -z "${EXIT_PC:-}" ]; then
      echo "FAIL[$FIXTURE pc]: missing _hybrid_enter_pc/_hybrid_exit_pc symbols"
      exit 1
    fi
    ;;
  *)
    echo "FAIL[$FIXTURE]: unknown TRIGGER_MODE=$TRIGGER_MODE (use csr|pc)"
    exit 1
    ;;
esac

STATE_QEMU=$(mktemp)
STATE_VSIM=$(mktemp)
SIM_LOG=$(mktemp)
trap 'rm -f "$STATE_QEMU" "$STATE_VSIM" "$SIM_LOG"' EXIT

PLUGIN_ARGS="outfile=$STATE_QEMU"
VSIM_TRIG_ARGS=()
if [ "$TRIGGER_MODE" = "pc" ]; then
  PLUGIN_ARGS="$PLUGIN_ARGS,enter_pc=0x$ENTER_PC"
  VSIM_TRIG_ARGS=(--hybrid-exit-pc "0x$EXIT_PC")
fi

echo "[$FIXTURE 1/3 mode=$TRIGGER_MODE] QEMU 1: drain entry state -> $STATE_QEMU"
# -cpu andes-ax45mpv,vlen=512:
#   - andes-ax45mpv gates Andes-specific CSRs (mcache_ctl, mmisc_ctl,
#     mxstatus, uitb, jvt, mdcause, ...) that the vsim-demo crt0/handler
#     touch; default rv64 illegal-traps on the first mcache_ctl write.
#   - vlen=512 pins QEMU's VLEN to 512 bits (vlenb=64), matching the vsim
#     RTL build (data/configs/ax45mpv_premium.cfg: NDS_VLEN 512) and
#     HYBRID_VLEN_BYTES_MAX=64 in include/hybrid/state_abi.h. The default
#     andes-ax45mpv has VLEN=1024 which silently truncates V-state in
#     the wire format. Non-V fixtures run identically under either VLEN.
# -semihosting-config enable=on,target=native: lets fixtures print from
# QEMU1 (phase 1) via newlib stdio (linked against libgloss_vh), matching
# the QEMU2 launch in qemu_handback.hpp. Phase 2 (vsim) is forbidden from
# doing any syscall -- vsim does not implement semihosting.
"$VSIM_QEMU" -M virt -cpu andes-ax45mpv,vlen=512 -bios none -nographic \
  -semihosting-config enable=on,target=native \
  -kernel "$VSIM_ELF" \
  -plugin "$VSIM_PLUGIN,$PLUGIN_ARGS" >/dev/null 2>&1

if [ ! -s "$STATE_QEMU" ]; then
  echo "FAIL[$FIXTURE]: QEMU 1 did not produce state"
  exit 1
fi

echo "[$FIXTURE 2/3 mode=$TRIGGER_MODE] vsim: resume + drain + spawn QEMU 2"
set +e
timeout 60 "$VSIM_BIN" "$VSIM_ELF" \
  --resume-from-qemu "$STATE_QEMU" \
  --handoff-out "$STATE_VSIM" \
  --qemu-binary "$VSIM_QEMU" \
  "${VSIM_TRIG_ARGS[@]}" >"$SIM_LOG" 2>&1
RC=$?
set -e

if [ "$RC" != "$EXPECT_RC" ]; then
  echo "FAIL[$FIXTURE]: expected exit $EXPECT_RC, got $RC"
  echo "--- sim log ---"
  cat "$SIM_LOG"
  exit 1
fi

echo "[$FIXTURE 3/3 mode=$TRIGGER_MODE] PASS: round-trip exit code $RC"

lines: # QEMU1 (phase 1) via newlib stdio (linked against libgloss_vh), matching
# the QEMU2 launch in qemu_handback.hpp. Phase 2 (vsim) is forbidden from
# doing any syscall -- vsim does not implement semihosting.
+# QEMU1 plugin drains state then exits 200 (== kExitHandoffToQemu) to
+# distinguish drain from workload completion (0). See F1 in ROADMAP.md.
+  echo "FAIL[$FIXTURE]: QEMU 1 expected exit 200 on drain, got $QEMU1_RC"
echo "FAIL[$FIXTURE]: QEMU 1 did not produce state"

toolUseResult.originalFile:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Tier-2 round-trip e2e: cross-simulator memory survival via shared mmap.
#
# QEMU 1, vsim, and QEMU 2 all map the same backing file. Phase A writes a
# pattern in QEMU 1; Phase B verifies + mutates in vsim; Phase C re-reads
# in QEMU 2. Memory must survive both handoffs.
#
# Required environment:
#   VSIM_BIN     path to sim_<cpu> binary (host-runnable)
#   VSIM_QEMU    path to qemu-system-riscv64
#   VSIM_PLUGIN  path to libhybrid_handoff.so

set -euo pipefail

SCRIPT_DIR=$(cd -- "$(dirname -- "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)
REPO_ROOT=$(cd -- "$SCRIPT_DIR/../.." && pwd)

# Fixture-parameterized: defaults match the original rt_shared_mem.S/elf
# fixture, but C-level Tier-2 fixtures (rt_c_hello, rt_c_printf, ...)
# reuse this harness by overriding FIXTURE/EXPECT_RC.
FIXTURE="${FIXTURE:-rt_shared_mem}"
EXPECT_RC="${EXPECT_RC:-0}"
TIMEOUT="${TIMEOUT:-60}"
TRIGGER_MODE="${TRIGGER_MODE:-csr}"

VSIM_BIN="${VSIM_BIN:-$REPO_ROOT/build/sim_ax45mpv_premium}"
VSIM_QEMU="${VSIM_QEMU:-/local/nick/qemu_v5/build/qemu-system-riscv64}"
VSIM_PLUGIN="${VSIM_PLUGIN:-$REPO_ROOT/hybrid/qemu_plugin/libhybrid_handoff.so}"
VSIM_ELF="${VSIM_ELF:-$REPO_ROOT/hybrid/test/${FIXTURE}.elf}"
NM="${NM:-/local/nick/SW_Release/build-ast540/build-toolchain/linux/nds64le-elf-newlib-v5d/bin/riscv64-unknown-elf-nm}"

for f in "$VSIM_BIN" "$VSIM_QEMU" "$VSIM_PLUGIN" "$VSIM_ELF"; do
  if [ ! -e "$f" ]; then
    echo "skip: $f not built; run 'make' under hybrid/test and hybrid/qemu_plugin"
    exit 77
  fi
done

case "$TRIGGER_MODE" in
  csr) ;;
  pc)
    if ! command -v "$NM" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
      echo "skip[$FIXTURE pc]: nm not found at $NM"
      exit 77
    fi
    # Capture nm output once: avoids racing awk's `exit` against nm's pipe
    # writes (large C-fixture symbol tables make nm SIGPIPE under pipefail).
    NM_OUT=$("$NM" --defined-only "$VSIM_ELF")
    ENTER_PC=$(printf '%s\n' "$NM_OUT" \
               | awk '$3=="_hybrid_enter_pc"{print $1}')
    EXIT_PC=$(printf '%s\n' "$NM_OUT" \
              | awk '$3=="_hybrid_exit_pc"{print $1}')
    if [ -z "${ENTER_PC:-}" ] || [ -z "${EXIT_PC:-}" ]; then
      echo "FAIL[$FIXTURE pc]: missing _hybrid_enter_pc/_hybrid_exit_pc symbols"
      exit 1
    fi
    ;;
  *)
    echo "FAIL[$FIXTURE]: unknown TRIGGER_MODE=$TRIGGER_MODE (use csr|pc)"
    exit 1
    ;;
esac

# 128 MB matches the QEMU virt machine default; vsim must use the same size.
SHARED_SIZE=$((128 * 1024 * 1024))
SHARED_MEM=$(mktemp -p /dev/shm vsim-rt-shared.XXXXXX)
STATE_QEMU=$(mktemp)
STATE_VSIM=$(mktemp)
SIM_LOG=$(mktemp)
trap 'rm -f "$SHARED_MEM" "$STATE_QEMU" "$STATE_VSIM" "$SIM_LOG"' EXIT

truncate -s "$SHARED_SIZE" "$SHARED_MEM"

PLUGIN_ARGS="outfile=$STATE_QEMU"
VSIM_TRIG_ARGS=()
if [ "$TRIGGER_MODE" = "pc" ]; then
  PLUGIN_ARGS="$PLUGIN_ARGS,enter_pc=0x$ENTER_PC"
  VSIM_TRIG_ARGS=(--hybrid-exit-pc "0x$EXIT_PC")
fi

echo "[$FIXTURE 1/3 mode=$TRIGGER_MODE] QEMU 1 (shared mmap): drain entry state"
# -cpu andes-ax45mpv,vlen=512: gates Andes-specific CSRs (mcache_ctl,
# mmisc_ctl, ...) and pins VLEN to 512 (vlenb=64) so the V state in the
# drain file matches HYBRID_VLEN_BYTES_MAX. Non-V fixtures run identically
# under either VLEN.
# -semihosting-config enable=on,target=native: lets fixtures print from
# QEMU1 (phase 1) via newlib stdio (linked against libgloss_vh), matching
# the QEMU2 launch in qemu_handback.hpp. Phase 2 (vsim) is forbidden from
# doing any syscall -- vsim does not implement semihosting.
"$VSIM_QEMU" \
  -object "memory-backend-file,id=mem,share=on,mem-path=$SHARED_MEM,size=$SHARED_SIZE" \
  -machine andes_ae350,memory-backend=mem \
  -cpu andes-ax45mpv,vlen=512 \
  -nographic \
  -semihosting-config enable=on,target=native \
  -bios "$VSIM_ELF" \
  -plugin "$VSIM_PLUGIN,$PLUGIN_ARGS" >/dev/null 2>&1

if [ ! -s "$STATE_QEMU" ]; then
  echo "FAIL[$FIXTURE]: QEMU 1 did not produce state"
  exit 1
fi

echo "[$FIXTURE 2/3 mode=$TRIGGER_MODE] vsim (shared mmap): resume + drain + spawn QEMU 2"
set +e
timeout "$TIMEOUT" "$VSIM_BIN" "$VSIM_ELF" \
  --resume-from-qemu "$STATE_QEMU" \
  --handoff-out "$STATE_VSIM" \
  --shared-mem-path "$SHARED_MEM" \
  --shared-mem-size "$SHARED_SIZE" \
  --shared-mem-base 0x00000000 \
  --qemu-binary "$VSIM_QEMU" \
  "${VSIM_TRIG_ARGS[@]}" >"$SIM_LOG" 2>&1
RC=$?
set -e

if [ "$RC" != "$EXPECT_RC" ]; then
  echo "FAIL[$FIXTURE]: expected exit $EXPECT_RC, got $RC"
  echo "--- sim log ---"
  cat "$SIM_LOG"
  exit 1
fi

echo "[$FIXTURE 3/3 mode=$TRIGGER_MODE] PASS: round-trip exit code $RC"

lines: # QEMU1 (phase 1) via newlib stdio (linked against libgloss_vh), matching
# the QEMU2 launch in qemu_handback.hpp. Phase 2 (vsim) is forbidden from
# doing any syscall -- vsim does not implement semihosting.
+# QEMU1 plugin drains state then exits 200 (== kExitHandoffToQemu) to
+# distinguish drain from workload completion (0). See F1 in ROADMAP.md.
+  echo "FAIL[$FIXTURE]: QEMU 1 expected exit 200 on drain, got $QEMU1_RC"
echo "FAIL[$FIXTURE]: QEMU 1 did not produce state"

#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Reproduces the SIGTTIN hang of QEMU 2 when vsim is launched from a
# non-foreground process group under a real pty.
#
# Without the spawn_qemu stdin redirect:
#   - timeout(1) places vsim+QEMU 2 in a new pgid that is NOT the pty's
#     foreground group.
#   - QEMU 2's -nographic stdio reads the pty, the kernel delivers SIGTTIN,
#     QEMU 2 stops, vsim's waitpid blocks, the outer timeout fires (rc=124).
#
# With the fix (child stdin -> /dev/null in spawn_qemu): no tty access,
# no SIGTTIN, vsim completes the round-trip normally (rc=3).

set -euo pipefail

SCRIPT_DIR=$(cd -- "$(dirname -- "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)
REPO_ROOT=$(cd -- "$SCRIPT_DIR/../.." && pwd)

VSIM_BIN="${VSIM_BIN:-$REPO_ROOT/build/sim_ax45mpv_premium}"
VSIM_QEMU="${VSIM_QEMU:-/local/nick/qemu_v5/build/qemu-system-riscv64}"
VSIM_PLUGIN="${VSIM_PLUGIN:-$REPO_ROOT/hybrid/qemu_plugin/libhybrid_handoff.so}"
VSIM_ELF="${VSIM_ELF:-$REPO_ROOT/hybrid/test/handoff_roundtrip.elf}"

for f in "$VSIM_BIN" "$VSIM_QEMU" "$VSIM_PLUGIN" "$VSIM_ELF"; do
  if [ ! -e "$f" ]; then
    echo "skip: $f not built"
    exit 77
  fi
done

if ! command -v script >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  echo "skip: script(1) not available"
  exit 77
fi

STATE_QEMU=$(mktemp)
STATE_VSIM=$(mktemp)
SIM_LOG=$(mktemp)
RCFILE=$(mktemp)
WRAPPER=$(mktemp --suffix=.sh)
chmod +x "$WRAPPER"
trap 'rm -f "$STATE_QEMU" "$STATE_VSIM" "$SIM_LOG" "$RCFILE" "$WRAPPER"' EXIT

"$VSIM_QEMU" -M virt -bios none -nographic \
  -kernel "$VSIM_ELF" \
  -plugin "$VSIM_PLUGIN,outfile=$STATE_QEMU" >/dev/null 2>&1

if [ ! -s "$STATE_QEMU" ]; then
  echo "FAIL[rt_pty_handback]: QEMU 1 did not produce state"
  exit 1
fi

# WRAPPER captures vsim's own exit into RCFILE so the test does not depend
# on script(1) propagating it. timeout(1) puts vsim+QEMU 2 in a fresh pgid
# that is NOT the pty's foreground group, which is the exact configuration
# that triggers the SIGTTIN/SIGTTOU stop on QEMU 2's tty access.
cat >"$WRAPPER" <<EOF
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set +e
timeout 20 "$VSIM_BIN" "$VSIM_ELF" \\
  --resume-from-qemu "$STATE_QEMU" \\
  --handoff-out "$STATE_VSIM" \\
  --qemu-binary "$VSIM_QEMU" >"$SIM_LOG" 2>&1
echo \$? >"$RCFILE"
EOF

START=$(date +%s)
script -qc "$WRAPPER" /dev/null >/dev/null
ELAPSED=$(($(date +%s) - START))

VSIM_RC=$(cat "$RCFILE" 2>/dev/null || echo "")
if [ "$VSIM_RC" != "3" ]; then
  echo "FAIL[rt_pty_handback]: expected vsim exit 3, got '$VSIM_RC' (elapsed ${ELAPSED}s)"
  echo "--- sim log ---"
  cat "$SIM_LOG"
  exit 1
fi

echo "[rt_pty_handback] PASS: vsim exit=$VSIM_RC under pty (elapsed ${ELAPSED}s)"

#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Negative test: --qemu-binary opens the gdbstub port but never responds to
# packets. Vsim's gdbstub Session must time out on the read side and exit
# non-zero promptly, not block forever waiting for an ack that never comes.

set -euo pipefail

SCRIPT_DIR=$(cd -- "$(dirname -- "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)
REPO_ROOT=$(cd -- "$SCRIPT_DIR/../.." && pwd)
STUB="$SCRIPT_DIR/qemu_silent_stub.py"

VSIM_BIN="${VSIM_BIN:-$REPO_ROOT/build/sim_ax45mpv_premium}"
VSIM_QEMU="${VSIM_QEMU:-/local/nick/qemu_v5/build/qemu-system-riscv64}"
VSIM_PLUGIN="${VSIM_PLUGIN:-$REPO_ROOT/hybrid/qemu_plugin/libhybrid_handoff.so}"
VSIM_ELF="${VSIM_ELF:-$REPO_ROOT/hybrid/test/handoff_roundtrip.elf}"

for f in "$VSIM_BIN" "$VSIM_QEMU" "$VSIM_PLUGIN" "$VSIM_ELF" "$STUB"; do
  if [ ! -e "$f" ]; then
    echo "skip: $f not built"
    exit 77
  fi
done

STATE_QEMU=$(mktemp)
STATE_VSIM=$(mktemp)
SIM_LOG=$(mktemp)
trap 'rm -f "$STATE_QEMU" "$STATE_VSIM" "$SIM_LOG"' EXIT

# Real QEMU 1 produces a valid entry-state snapshot so vsim reaches the
# exit-csrwi drain and tries to spawn the (silent) handback peer.
"$VSIM_QEMU" -M virt -bios none -nographic \
  -kernel "$VSIM_ELF" \
  -plugin "$VSIM_PLUGIN,outfile=$STATE_QEMU" >/dev/null 2>&1

if [ ! -s "$STATE_QEMU" ]; then
  echo "FAIL[rt_gdbstub_timeout]: QEMU 1 did not produce state"
  exit 1
fi

START=$(date +%s)
set +e
timeout 30 "$VSIM_BIN" "$VSIM_ELF" \
  --resume-from-qemu "$STATE_QEMU" \
  --handoff-out "$STATE_VSIM" \
  --qemu-binary "$STUB" >"$SIM_LOG" 2>&1
RC=$?
set -e
ELAPSED=$(($(date +%s) - START))

if [ "$RC" -eq 0 ] || [ "$RC" -eq 200 ]; then
  echo "FAIL[rt_gdbstub_timeout]: expected non-zero non-200 exit, got $RC"
  cat "$SIM_LOG"
  exit 1

toolUseResult.originalFile:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Negative test: --qemu-binary points at a missing file. Vsim's drain must
# still fire and the spawn-fail must propagate as exit 127 (the standard
# exec-failure code), not as a 5 s connect_loopback timeout that masquerades
# as something else, and not as a hang that ties up CTest.

set -euo pipefail

SCRIPT_DIR=$(cd -- "$(dirname -- "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)
REPO_ROOT=$(cd -- "$SCRIPT_DIR/../.." && pwd)

VSIM_BIN="${VSIM_BIN:-$REPO_ROOT/build/sim_ax45mpv_premium}"
VSIM_QEMU="${VSIM_QEMU:-/local/nick/qemu_v5/build/qemu-system-riscv64}"
VSIM_PLUGIN="${VSIM_PLUGIN:-$REPO_ROOT/hybrid/qemu_plugin/libhybrid_handoff.so}"
VSIM_ELF="${VSIM_ELF:-$REPO_ROOT/hybrid/test/handoff_roundtrip.elf}"

for f in "$VSIM_BIN" "$VSIM_QEMU" "$VSIM_PLUGIN" "$VSIM_ELF"; do
  if [ ! -e "$f" ]; then
    echo "skip: $f not built"
    exit 77
  fi
done

STATE_QEMU=$(mktemp)
STATE_VSIM=$(mktemp)
SIM_LOG=$(mktemp)
trap 'rm -f "$STATE_QEMU" "$STATE_VSIM" "$SIM_LOG"' EXIT

# Phase A: real QEMU 1 produces a valid entry-state snapshot so vsim has
# something to resume from and will reach the exit-csrwi drain.
"$VSIM_QEMU" -M virt -bios none -nographic \
  -kernel "$VSIM_ELF" \
  -plugin "$VSIM_PLUGIN,outfile=$STATE_QEMU" >/dev/null 2>&1

if [ ! -s "$STATE_QEMU" ]; then
  echo "FAIL[rt_no_qemu_binary]: QEMU 1 did not produce state"
  exit 1
fi

START=$(date +%s)
set +e
timeout 30 "$VSIM_BIN" "$VSIM_ELF" \
  --resume-from-qemu "$STATE_QEMU" \
  --handoff-out "$STATE_VSIM" \
  --qemu-binary /nonexistent/qemu-system-riscv64 >"$SIM_LOG" 2>&1
RC=$?
set -e
ELAPSED=$(($(date +%s) - START))

if [ "$RC" -ne 127 ]; then
  echo "FAIL[rt_no_qemu_binary]: expected exit 127 (exec failure), got $RC"
  echo "elapsed=${ELAPSED}s"
  echo "--- sim log ---"
  cat "$SIM_LOG"
  exit 1
fi

if [ "$ELAPSED" -gt 15 ]; then
  echo "FAIL[rt_no_qemu_binary]: vsim took ${ELAPSED}s (>15s suggests a hang)"
  exit 1
fi

# No zombie left: any leftover child of this shell would show up here.
if jobs -r | grep -q .; then
  echo "FAIL[rt_no_qemu_binary]: background jobs still running"
  exit 1
fi

echo "[rt_no_qemu_binary] PASS: vsim exit=$RC elapsed=${ELAPSED}s"

file.content:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Test: QEMU plugin drains vxsat / vxrm / vcsr into the state file.
#
# Runs the probe_v_csr fixture (sets vxrm=1, vxsat=1 before QEMU1 drain)
# and verifies the state binary holds those values at the ABI-locked offsets.
#
# Offsets from state_abi.h / state_abi_check.h (HYBRID_VLEN_BYTES_MAX=64):
#   vxsat = 1320 + 32*64 + 0  = 3368
#   vxrm  = 1320 + 32*64 + 8  = 3376
#   vcsr  = 1320 + 32*64 + 16 = 3384
#
# Expected values after the fixture: vxsat=1, vxrm=1, vcsr=3
#   vcsr = (vxrm << 1) | vxsat = (1 << 1) | 1 = 3
#
# Returns 0 (PASS) or 1 (FAIL). Exit code 77 skips when artifacts are absent.

set -euo pipefail

ELF_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "$0")/../../hybrid/test" && pwd)"
PLUGIN_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "$0")/../../../qemu_plugin" && pwd)"
QEMU="${QEMU:-$(cd "$(dirname "$0")/../../../build/qemu" && pwd)/qemu-system-riscv64}"

ELF="$ELF_DIR/probe_v_csr.elf"
PLUGIN="$PLUGIN_DIR/libhybrid_handoff.so"

[[ -x "$QEMU" ]]    || { echo "SKIP: no QEMU at $QEMU";   exit 77; }
[[ -f "$PLUGIN" ]]  || { echo "SKIP: no plugin at $PLUGIN"; exit 77; }
[[ -f "$ELF" ]]     || { echo "SKIP: no ELF at $ELF";     exit 77; }

STATE=$(mktemp)
trap 'rm -f "$STATE"' EXIT

"$QEMU" -M virt -cpu andes-ax45mpv,vlen=512 -bios none -nographic \
  -kernel "$ELF" -plugin "$PLUGIN,outfile=$STATE" 2>/dev/null
rc=$?
if [[ $rc -ne 0 ]]; then
  echo "FAIL: QEMU exited with rc=$rc"
  exit 1
fi

# Read 8-byte little-endian uint64 at a byte offset from the state file.
read_u64_le() {
  local off=$1
  od -A n -j "$off" -N 8 -t u8 "$STATE" | tr -d ' \n'
}

got_vxsat=$(read_u64_le 3368)
got_vxrm=$(read_u64_le  3376)
got_vcsr=$(read_u64_le  3384)

echo "vxsat=$got_vxsat (want 1)"
echo "vxrm=$got_vxrm  (want 1)"
echo "vcsr=$got_vcsr   (want 3)"

fail=0
[[ "$got_vxsat" == "1" ]] || { echo "FAIL: vxsat=$got_vxsat != 1"; fail=1; }
[[ "$got_vxrm"  == "1" ]] || { echo "FAIL: vxrm=$got_vxrm != 1";  fail=1; }
[[ "$got_vcsr"  == "3" ]] || { echo "FAIL: vcsr=$got_vcsr != 3";   fail=1; }

if [[ $fail -eq 0 ]]; then
  echo "PASS"
  exit 0
else
  exit 1
fi

attachment.snippet:
1	#!/usr/bin/env bash
2	# Tier-2 round-trip e2e: cross-simulator memory survival via shared mmap.
3	#
4	# QEMU 1, vsim, and QEMU 2 all map the same backing file. Phase A writes a
5	# pattern in QEMU 1; Phase B verifies + mutates in vsim; Phase C re-reads
6	# in QEMU 2. Memory must survive both handoffs.
7	#
8	# Required environment:
9	#   VSIM_BIN     path to sim_<cpu> binary (host-runnable)
10	#   VSIM_QEMU    path to qemu-system-riscv64
11	#   VSIM_PLUGIN  path to libhybrid_handoff.so
12	
13	set -euo pipefail
14	
15	SCRIPT_DIR=$(cd -- "$(dirname -- "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)
16	REPO_ROOT=$(cd -- "$SCRIPT_DIR/../.." && pwd)
17	
18	# Fixture-parameterized: defaults match the original rt_shared_mem.S/elf
19	# fixture, but C-level Tier-2 fixtures (rt_c_hello, rt_c_printf, ...)
20	# reuse this harness by overriding FIXTURE/EXPECT_RC.
21	FIXTURE="${FIXTURE:-rt_shared_mem}"
22	EXPECT_RC="${EXPECT_RC:-0}"
23	TIMEOUT="${TIMEOUT:-60}"
24	TRIGGER_MODE="${TRIGGER_MODE:-csr}"
25	
26	VSIM_BIN="${VSIM_BIN:-$REPO_ROOT/build/sim_ax45mpv_premium}"
27	VSIM_QEMU="${VSIM_QEMU:-/local/nick/qemu_v5/build/qemu-system-riscv64}"
28	VSIM_PLUGIN="${VSIM_PLUGIN:-$REPO_ROOT/hybrid/qemu_plugin/libhybrid_handoff.so}"
29	VSIM_ELF="${VSIM_ELF:-$REPO_ROOT/hybrid/test/${FIXTURE}.elf}"
30	NM="${NM:-/local/nick/SW_Release/build-ast540/build-toolchain/linux/nds64le-elf-newlib-v5d/bin/riscv64-unknown-elf-nm}"
31	
32	for f in "$VSIM_BIN" "$VSIM_QEMU" "$VSIM_PLUGIN" "$VSIM_ELF"; do
33	  if [ ! -e "$f" ]; then
34	    echo "skip: $f not built; run 'make' under hybrid/test and hybrid/qemu_plugin"
35	    exit 77
36	  fi
37	done
38	
39	case "$TRIGGER_MODE" in
40	  csr) ;;
41	  pc)
42	    if ! command -v "$NM" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
43	      echo "skip[$FIXTURE pc]: nm not found at $NM"
44	      exit 77
45	    fi
46	    # Capture nm output once: avoids racing awk's `exit` against nm's pipe
47	    # writes (large C-fixture symbol tables make nm SIGPIPE under pipefail).
48	    NM_OUT=$("$NM" --defined-only "$VSIM_ELF")
49	    ENTER_PC=$(printf '%s\n' "$NM_OUT" \
50	               | awk '$3=="_hybrid_enter_pc"{print $1}')
51	    EXIT_PC=$(printf '%s\n' "$NM_OUT" \
52	              | awk '$3=="_hybrid_exit_pc"{print $1}')
53	    if [ -z "${ENTER_PC:-}" ] || [ -z "${EXIT_PC:-}" ]; then
54	      echo "FAIL[$FIXTURE pc]: missing _hybrid_enter_pc/_hybrid_exit_pc symbols"
55	      exit 1
56	    fi
57	    ;;
58	  *)
59	    echo "FAIL[$FIXTURE]: unknown TRIGGER_MODE=$TRIGGER_MODE (use csr|pc)"
60	    exit 1
61	    ;;
62	esac
63	
64	# 128 MB matches the QEMU virt machine default; vsim must use the same size.
65	SHARED_SIZE=$((128 * 1024 * 1024))
66	SHARED_MEM=$(mktemp -p /dev/shm vsim-rt-shared.XXXXXX)
67	STATE_QEMU=$(mktemp)
68	STATE_VSIM=$(mktemp)
69	SIM_LOG=$(mktemp)
70	trap 'rm -f "$SHARED_MEM" "$STATE_QEMU" "$STATE_VSIM" "$SIM_LOG"' EXIT
71	
72	truncate -s "$SHARED_SIZE" "$SHARED_MEM"
73	
74	PLUGIN_ARGS="outfile=$STATE_QEMU"
75	VSIM_TRIG_ARGS=()
76	if [ "$TRIGGER_MODE" = "pc" ]; then
77	  PLUGIN_ARGS="$PLUGIN_ARGS,enter_pc=0x$ENTER_PC"
78	  VSIM_TRIG_ARGS=(--hybrid-exit-pc "0x$EXIT_PC")
79	fi
80	
81	echo "[$FIXTURE 1/3 mode=$TRIGGER_MODE] QEMU 1 (shared mmap): drain entry state"
82	# -cpu andes-ax45mpv,vlen=512: gates Andes-specific CSRs (mcache_ctl,
83	# mmisc_ctl, ...) and pins VLEN to 512 (vlenb=64) so the V state in the
84	# drain file matches HYBRID_VLEN_BYTES_MAX. Non-V fixtures run identically
85	# under either VLEN.
86	# -semihosting-config enable=on,target=native: lets fixtures print from
87	# QEMU1 (phase 1) via newlib stdio (linked against libgloss_vh), matching
88	# the QEMU2 launch in qemu_handback.hpp. Phase 2 (vsim) is forbidden from
89	# doing any syscall -- vsim does not implement semihosting.
90	"$VSIM_QEMU" \
91	  -object "memory-backend-file,id=mem,share=on,mem-path=$SHARED_MEM,size=$SHARED_SIZE" \
92	  -machine virt,memory-backend=mem \
93	  -cpu andes-ax45mpv,vlen=512 \
94	  -bios none -nographic \
95	  -semihosting-config enable=on,target=native \
96	  -kernel "$VSIM_ELF" \
97	  -plugin "$VSIM_PLUGIN,$PLUGIN_ARGS" >/dev/null 2>&1
98	
99	if [ ! -s "$STATE_QEMU" ]; then
100	  echo "FAIL[$FIXTURE]: QEMU 1 did not produce state"
101	  exit 1
102	fi
103	
104	echo "[$FIXTURE 2/3 mode=$TRIGGER_MODE] vsim (shared mmap): resume + drain + spawn QEMU 2"
105	set +e
106	timeout "$TIMEOUT" "$VSIM_BIN" "$VSIM_ELF" \
107	  --resume-from-qemu "$STATE_QEMU" \
108	  --handoff-out "$STATE_VSIM" \
109	  --shared-mem-path "$SHARED_MEM" \
110	  --shared-mem-size "$SHARED_SIZE" \
111	  --qemu-binary "$VSIM_QEMU" \
112	  "${VSIM_TRIG_ARGS[@]}" >"$SIM_LOG" 2>&1
113	RC=$?
114	set -e
115	
116	if [ "$RC" != "$EXPECT_RC" ]; then
117	  echo "FAIL[$FIXTURE]: expected exit $EXPECT_RC, got $RC"
118	  echo "--- sim log ---"
119	  cat "$SIM_LOG"
120	  exit 1
121	fi
122	
123	echo "[$FIXTURE 3/3 mode=$TRIGGER_MODE] PASS: round-trip exit code $RC"

diff --git a/tests/hybrid/rt_shared_mem.sh b/tests/hybrid/rt_shared_mem.sh
index 348be23..8ebe5d2 100755
--- a/tests/hybrid/rt_shared_mem.sh
+++ b/tests/hybrid/rt_shared_mem.sh
@@ -87,14 +87,24 @@ echo "[$FIXTURE 1/3 mode=$TRIGGER_MODE] QEMU 1 (shared mmap): drain entry state"
 # QEMU1 (phase 1) via newlib stdio (linked against libgloss_vh), matching
 # the QEMU2 launch in qemu_handback.hpp. Phase 2 (vsim) is forbidden from
 # doing any syscall -- vsim does not implement semihosting.
+# QEMU1 plugin drains state then exits 200 (== kExitHandoffToQemu) to
+# distinguish drain from workload completion (0). See F1 in ROADMAP.md.
+set +e
 "$VSIM_QEMU" \
   -object "memory-backend-file,id=mem,share=on,mem-path=$SHARED_MEM,size=$SHARED_SIZE" \
-  -machine virt,memory-backend=mem \
+  -machine andes_ae350,memory-backend=mem \
   -cpu andes-ax45mpv,vlen=512 \
-  -bios none -nographic \
+  -nographic \
   -semihosting-config enable=on,target=native \
-  -kernel "$VSIM_ELF" \
+  -bios "$VSIM_ELF" \
   -plugin "$VSIM_PLUGIN,$PLUGIN_ARGS" >/dev/null 2>&1
+QEMU1_RC=$?

file.content:
echo "[$FIXTURE 1/3 mode=$TRIGGER_MODE] QEMU 1 (shared mmap): drain entry state"
# -cpu andes-ax45mpv,vlen=512: gates Andes-specific CSRs (mcache_ctl,
# mmisc_ctl, ...) and pins VLEN to 512 (vlenb=64) so the V state in the
# drain file matches HYBRID_VLEN_BYTES_MAX. Non-V fixtures run identically
# under either VLEN.
# -semihosting-config enable=on,target=native: lets fixtures print from
# QEMU1 (phase 1) via newlib stdio (linked against libgloss_vh), matching
# the QEMU2 launch in qemu_handback.hpp. Phase 2 (vsim) is forbidden from
# doing any syscall -- vsim does not implement semihosting.
"$VSIM_QEMU" \
  -object "memory-backend-file,id=mem,share=on,mem-path=$SHARED_MEM,size=$SHARED_SIZE" \
  -machine virt,memory-backend=mem \
  -cpu andes-ax45mpv,vlen=512 \
  -bios none -nographic \
  -semihosting-config enable=on,target=native \
  -kernel "$VSIM_ELF" \
  -plugin "$VSIM_PLUGIN,$PLUGIN_ARGS" >/dev/null 2>&1

if [ ! -s "$STATE_QEMU" ]; then
  echo "FAIL[$FIXTURE]: QEMU 1 did not produce state"
  exit 1
fi

echo "[$FIXTURE 2/3 mode=$TRIGGER_MODE] vsim (shared mmap): resume + drain + spawn QEMU 2"
set +e
timeout "$TIMEOUT" "$VSIM_BIN" "$VSIM_ELF" \
  --resume-from-qemu "$STATE_QEMU" \
  --handoff-out "$STATE_VSIM" \
  --shared-mem-path "$SHARED_MEM" \
  --shared-mem-size "$SHARED_SIZE" \
  --qemu-binary "$VSIM_QEMU" \
  "${VSIM_TRIG_ARGS[@]}" >"$SIM_LOG" 2>&1
RC=$?
set -e

toolUseResult.originalFile:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Tier-2 round-trip e2e: cross-simulator memory survival via shared mmap.
#
# QEMU 1, vsim, and QEMU 2 all map the same backing file. Phase A writes a
# pattern in QEMU 1; Phase B verifies + mutates in vsim; Phase C re-reads
# in QEMU 2. Memory must survive both handoffs.
#
# Required environment:
#   VSIM_BIN     path to sim_<cpu> binary (host-runnable)
#   VSIM_QEMU    path to qemu-system-riscv64
#   VSIM_PLUGIN  path to libhybrid_handoff.so

set -euo pipefail

SCRIPT_DIR=$(cd -- "$(dirname -- "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)
REPO_ROOT=$(cd -- "$SCRIPT_DIR/../.." && pwd)

# Fixture-parameterized: defaults match the original rt_shared_mem.S/elf
# fixture, but C-level Tier-2 fixtures (rt_c_hello, rt_c_printf, ...)
# reuse this harness by overriding FIXTURE/EXPECT_RC.
FIXTURE="${FIXTURE:-rt_shared_mem}"
EXPECT_RC="${EXPECT_RC:-0}"
TIMEOUT="${TIMEOUT:-60}"
TRIGGER_MODE="${TRIGGER_MODE:-csr}"

VSIM_BIN="${VSIM_BIN:-$REPO_ROOT/build/sim_ax45mpv_premium}"
VSIM_QEMU="${VSIM_QEMU:-/local/nick/qemu_v5/build/qemu-system-riscv64}"
VSIM_PLUGIN="${VSIM_PLUGIN:-$REPO_ROOT/hybrid/qemu_plugin/libhybrid_handoff.so}"
VSIM_ELF="${VSIM_ELF:-$REPO_ROOT/hybrid/test/${FIXTURE}.elf}"
NM="${NM:-/local/nick/SW_Release/build-ast540/build-toolchain/linux/nds64le-elf-newlib-v5d/bin/riscv64-unknown-elf-nm}"

for f in "$VSIM_BIN" "$VSIM_QEMU" "$VSIM_PLUGIN" "$VSIM_ELF"; do
  if [ ! -e "$f" ]; then
    echo "skip: $f not built; run 'make' under hybrid/test and hybrid/qemu_plugin"
    exit 77
  fi
done

case "$TRIGGER_MODE" in
  csr) ;;
  pc)
    if ! command -v "$NM" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
      echo "skip[$FIXTURE pc]: nm not found at $NM"
      exit 77
    fi
    # Capture nm output once: avoids racing awk's `exit` against nm's pipe
    # writes (large C-fixture symbol tables make nm SIGPIPE under pipefail).
    NM_OUT=$("$NM" --defined-only "$VSIM_ELF")
    ENTER_PC=$(printf '%s\n' "$NM_OUT" \
               | awk '$3=="_hybrid_enter_pc"{print $1}')
    EXIT_PC=$(printf '%s\n' "$NM_OUT" \
              | awk '$3=="_hybrid_exit_pc"{print $1}')
    if [ -z "${ENTER_PC:-}" ] || [ -z "${EXIT_PC:-}" ]; then
      echo "FAIL[$FIXTURE pc]: missing _hybrid_enter_pc/_hybrid_exit_pc symbols"
      exit 1
    fi
    ;;
  *)
    echo "FAIL[$FIXTURE]: unknown TRIGGER_MODE=$TRIGGER_MODE (use csr|pc)"
    exit 1
    ;;
esac

# 128 MB matches the QEMU virt machine default; vsim must use the same size.
SHARED_SIZE=$((128 * 1024 * 1024))
SHARED_MEM=$(mktemp -p /dev/shm vsim-rt-shared.XXXXXX)
STATE_QEMU=$(mktemp)
STATE_VSIM=$(mktemp)
SIM_LOG=$(mktemp)
trap 'rm -f "$SHARED_MEM" "$STATE_QEMU" "$STATE_VSIM" "$SIM_LOG"' EXIT

truncate -s "$SHARED_SIZE" "$SHARED_MEM"

PLUGIN_ARGS="outfile=$STATE_QEMU"
VSIM_TRIG_ARGS=()
if [ "$TRIGGER_MODE" = "pc" ]; then
  PLUGIN_ARGS="$PLUGIN_ARGS,enter_pc=0x$ENTER_PC"
  VSIM_TRIG_ARGS=(--hybrid-exit-pc "0x$EXIT_PC")
fi

echo "[$FIXTURE 1/3 mode=$TRIGGER_MODE] QEMU 1 (shared mmap): drain entry state"
# -cpu andes-ax45mpv,vlen=512: gates Andes-specific CSRs (mcache_ctl,
# mmisc_ctl, ...) and pins VLEN to 512 (vlenb=64) so the V state in the
# drain file matches HYBRID_VLEN_BYTES_MAX. Non-V fixtures run identically
# under either VLEN.
# -semihosting-config enable=on,target=native: lets fixtures print from
# QEMU1 (phase 1) via newlib stdio (linked against libgloss_vh), matching
# the QEMU2 launch in qemu_handback.hpp. Phase 2 (vsim) is forbidden from
# doing any syscall -- vsim does not implement semihosting.
"$VSIM_QEMU" \
  -object "memory-backend-file,id=mem,share=on,mem-path=$SHARED_MEM,size=$SHARED_SIZE" \
  -machine virt,memory-backend=mem \
  -cpu andes-ax45mpv,vlen=512 \
  -bios none -nographic \
  -semihosting-config enable=on,target=native \
  -kernel "$VSIM_ELF" \
  -plugin "$VSIM_PLUGIN,$PLUGIN_ARGS" >/dev/null 2>&1

if [ ! -s "$STATE_QEMU" ]; then
  echo "FAIL[$FIXTURE]: QEMU 1 did not produce state"
  exit 1
fi

echo "[$FIXTURE 2/3 mode=$TRIGGER_MODE] vsim (shared mmap): resume + drain + spawn QEMU 2"
set +e
timeout "$TIMEOUT" "$VSIM_BIN" "$VSIM_ELF" \
  --resume-from-qemu "$STATE_QEMU" \
  --handoff-out "$STATE_VSIM" \
  --shared-mem-path "$SHARED_MEM" \
  --shared-mem-size "$SHARED_SIZE" \
  --qemu-binary "$VSIM_QEMU" \
  "${VSIM_TRIG_ARGS[@]}" >"$SIM_LOG" 2>&1
RC=$?
set -e

if [ "$RC" != "$EXPECT_RC" ]; then
  echo "FAIL[$FIXTURE]: expected exit $EXPECT_RC, got $RC"
  echo "--- sim log ---"
  cat "$SIM_LOG"
  exit 1
fi

echo "[$FIXTURE 3/3 mode=$TRIGGER_MODE] PASS: round-trip exit code $RC"

lines: # QEMU1 (phase 1) via newlib stdio (linked against libgloss_vh), matching
# the QEMU2 launch in qemu_handback.hpp. Phase 2 (vsim) is forbidden from
# doing any syscall -- vsim does not implement semihosting.
+# QEMU1 plugin drains state then exits 200 (== kExitHandoffToQemu) to
+# distinguish drain from workload completion (0). See F1 in ROADMAP.md.
+  echo "FAIL[$FIXTURE]: QEMU 1 expected exit 200 on drain, got $QEMU1_RC"
echo "FAIL[$FIXTURE]: QEMU 1 did not produce state"

 # QEMU1 (phase 1) via newlib stdio (linked against libgloss_vh), matching
 # the QEMU2 launch in qemu_handback.hpp. Phase 2 (vsim) is forbidden from
 # doing any syscall -- vsim does not implement semihosting.
+# QEMU1 plugin drains state then exits 200 (== kExitHandoffToQemu) to
+# distinguish drain from workload completion (0). See F1 in ROADMAP.md.
+set +e
 "$VSIM_QEMU" \
   -object "memory-backend-file,id=mem,share=on,mem-path=$SHARED_MEM,size=$SHARED_SIZE" \
   -machine virt,memory-backend=mem \
@@ -95,6 +98,13 @@ echo "[$FIXTURE 1/3 mode=$TRIGGER_MODE] QEMU 1 (shared mmap): drain entry state"
   -semihosting-config enable=on,target=native \
   -kernel "$VSIM_ELF" \
   -plugin "$VSIM_PLUGIN,$PLUGIN_ARGS" >/dev/null 2>&1
+QEMU1_RC=$?
+set -e
+
+if [ "$QEMU1_RC" -ne 200 ]; then
+  echo "FAIL[$FIXTURE]: QEMU 1 expected exit 200 on drain, got $QEMU1_RC"
+  exit 1
+fi
 
 if [ ! -s "$STATE_QEMU" ]; then
   echo "FAIL[$FIXTURE]: QEMU 1 did not produce state"

commit 816c9920eb4e1ffacbc7bd1f506fccee008a8f5e
Author: Nick Hung <nick@andestech.com>
Date:   Tue May 12 09:21:57 2026 +0800

    behavioral: tier-2 C-fixture path; skip redundant ELF reload
    
    - Link C fixtures against the Andes toolchain's libgloss_vh.a so
      newlib stdio bottoms out in RISC-V semihosting; remove the
      project-specific libgloss/htif/syscall stubs and the unused
      ecall syscall-dispatch table in runtime/handler.c.
    - Establish the phase-2 rule: the span between enter_vsim() and
      exit_vsim() runs on vsim and MUST NOT issue any syscall (vsim
      does not implement semihosting). Drain markers renamed from
      rt_phase_qemu1_drain/rt_phase_vsim_drain to enter_vsim/exit_vsim.
      QEMU 1 and QEMU 2 launches gain -semihosting-config target=native
      to intercept the magic triple before mtvec.
    - In tier-2 hybrid resume (--shared-mem-path + --resume-from-qemu),
      vsim skips read_elf_into_memory and QEMU 2 drops -kernel: the
      shared memory-backend-file already holds the program from QEMU 1's
      initial -kernel load, and re-loading would std::fill_n the .bss

input.description: Show rt_shared_mem.sh diff in latest commit

content.content:
commit 816c9920eb4e1ffacbc7bd1f506fccee008a8f5e
Author: Nick Hung <nick@andestech.com>
Date:   Tue May 12 09:21:57 2026 +0800

    behavioral: tier-2 C-fixture path; skip redundant ELF reload
    
    - Link C fixtures against the Andes toolchain's libgloss_vh.a so
      newlib stdio bottoms out in RISC-V semihosting; remove the
      project-specific libgloss/htif/syscall stubs and the unused
      ecall syscall-dispatch table in runtime/handler.c.
    - Establish the phase-2 rule: the span between enter_vsim() and
      exit_vsim() runs on vsim and MUST NOT issue any syscall (vsim
      does not implement semihosting). Drain markers renamed from
      rt_phase_qemu1_drain/rt_phase_vsim_drain to enter_vsim/exit_vsim.
      QEMU 1 and QEMU 2 launches gain -semihosting-config target=native
      to intercept the magic triple before mtvec.
    - In tier-2 hybrid resume (--shared-mem-path + --resume-from-qemu),
      vsim skips read_elf_into_memory and QEMU 2 drops -kernel: the
      shared memory-backend-file already holds the program from QEMU 1's
      initial -kernel load, and re-loading would std::fill_n the .bss
      tail of each PT_LOAD over phase-1's writes. vsim still parses the
      ELF to set the reset vector (the hart fetches a few instructions
      between sim.reset() and the DMI halt; an unmapped PC there leaves
      the hart in an unrecoverable state).
    - Add rt_c_static_buf regression: a static uint32_t buf[64] seeded
      in phase 1, no-op in phase 2, verified in phase 3. Fails on the
      HEAD code path; passes with the skip-reload fix.
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

diff --git a/tests/hybrid/rt_shared_mem.sh b/tests/hybrid/rt_shared_mem.sh
index 521df1a..348be23 100755
--- a/tests/hybrid/rt_shared_mem.sh
+++ b/tests/hybrid/rt_shared_mem.sh
@@ -16,7 +16,7 @@ SCRIPT_DIR=$(cd -- "$(dirname -- "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)
 REPO_ROOT=$(cd -- "$SCRIPT_DIR/../.." && pwd)
 
 # Fixture-parameterized: defaults match the original rt_shared_mem.S/elf
-# fixture, but C-level Tier-2 fixtures (rt_c_hello, rt_c_printf, rt_c_file_io)
+# fixture, but C-level Tier-2 fixtures (rt_c_hello, rt_c_printf, ...)
 # reuse this harness by overriding FIXTURE/EXPECT_RC.
 FIXTURE="${FIXTURE:-rt_shared_mem}"
 EXPECT_RC="${EXPECT_RC:-0}"
@@ -83,11 +83,16 @@ echo "[$FIXTURE 1/3 mode=$TRIGGER_MODE] QEMU 1 (shared mmap): drain entry state"
 # mmisc_ctl, ...) and pins VLEN to 512 (vlenb=64) so the V state in the
 # drain file matches HYBRID_VLEN_BYTES_MAX. Non-V fixtures run identically
 # under either VLEN.
+# -semihosting-config enable=on,target=native: lets fixtures print from
+# QEMU1 (phase 1) via newlib stdio (linked against libgloss_vh), matching
+# the QEMU2 launch in qemu_handback.hpp. Phase 2 (vsim) is forbidden from
+# doing any syscall -- vsim does not implement semihosting.
 "$VSIM_QEMU" \
   -object "memory-backend-file,id=mem,share=on,mem-path=$SHARED_MEM,size=$SHARED_SIZE" \
   -machine virt,memory-backend=mem \
   -cpu andes-ax45mpv,vlen=512 \
   -bios none -nographic \
+  -semihosting-config enable=on,target=native \
   -kernel "$VSIM_ELF" \
   -plugin "$VSIM_PLUGIN,$PLUGIN_ARGS" >/dev/null 2>&1

QEMU1_RC=$?
set -e
toolUseResult.originalFile:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Tier-2 round-trip e2e: cross-simulator memory survival via shared mmap.
#
# QEMU 1, vsim, and QEMU 2 all map the same backing file. Phase A writes a
# pattern in QEMU 1; Phase B verifies + mutates in vsim; Phase C re-reads
# in QEMU 2. Memory must survive both handoffs.
#
# Required environment:
#   VSIM_BIN     path to sim_<cpu> binary (host-runnable)
#   VSIM_QEMU    path to qemu-system-riscv64
#   VSIM_PLUGIN  path to libhybrid_handoff.so

set -euo pipefail

SCRIPT_DIR=$(cd -- "$(dirname -- "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)
REPO_ROOT=$(cd -- "$SCRIPT_DIR/../.." && pwd)

# Fixture-parameterized: defaults match the original rt_shared_mem.S/elf
# fixture, but C-level Tier-2 fixtures (rt_c_hello, rt_c_printf, ...)
# reuse this harness by overriding FIXTURE/EXPECT_RC.
FIXTURE="${FIXTURE:-rt_shared_mem}"
EXPECT_RC="${EXPECT_RC:-0}"
TIMEOUT="${TIMEOUT:-60}"
TRIGGER_MODE="${TRIGGER_MODE:-csr}"

VSIM_BIN="${VSIM_BIN:-$REPO_ROOT/build/sim_ax45mpv_premium}"
VSIM_QEMU="${VSIM_QEMU:-/local/nick/qemu_v5/build/qemu-system-riscv64}"
VSIM_PLUGIN="${VSIM_PLUGIN:-$REPO_ROOT/hybrid/qemu_plugin/libhybrid_handoff.so}"
VSIM_ELF="${VSIM_ELF:-$REPO_ROOT/hybrid/test/${FIXTURE}.elf}"
NM="${NM:-/local/nick/SW_Release/build-ast540/build-toolchain/linux/nds64le-elf-newlib-v5d/bin/riscv64-unknown-elf-nm}"

for f in "$VSIM_BIN" "$VSIM_QEMU" "$VSIM_PLUGIN" "$VSIM_ELF"; do
  if [ ! -e "$f" ]; then
    echo "skip: $f not built; run 'make' under hybrid/test and hybrid/qemu_plugin"
    exit 77
  fi
done

case "$TRIGGER_MODE" in
  csr) ;;
  pc)
    if ! command -v "$NM" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
      echo "skip[$FIXTURE pc]: nm not found at $NM"
      exit 77
    fi
    # Capture nm output once: avoids racing awk's `exit` against nm's pipe
    # writes (large C-fixture symbol tables make nm SIGPIPE under pipefail).
    NM_OUT=$("$NM" --defined-only "$VSIM_ELF")
    ENTER_PC=$(printf '%s\n' "$NM_OUT" \
               | awk '$3=="_hybrid_enter_pc"{print $1}')
    EXIT_PC=$(printf '%s\n' "$NM_OUT" \
              | awk '$3=="_hybrid_exit_pc"{print $1}')
    if [ -z "${ENTER_PC:-}" ] || [ -z "${EXIT_PC:-}" ]; then
      echo "FAIL[$FIXTURE pc]: missing _hybrid_enter_pc/_hybrid_exit_pc symbols"
      exit 1
    fi
    ;;
  *)
    echo "FAIL[$FIXTURE]: unknown TRIGGER_MODE=$TRIGGER_MODE (use csr|pc)"
    exit 1
    ;;
esac

# 128 MB matches the QEMU virt machine default; vsim must use the same size.
SHARED_SIZE=$((128 * 1024 * 1024))
SHARED_MEM=$(mktemp -p /dev/shm vsim-rt-shared.XXXXXX)
STATE_QEMU=$(mktemp)
STATE_VSIM=$(mktemp)
SIM_LOG=$(mktemp)
trap 'rm -f "$SHARED_MEM" "$STATE_QEMU" "$STATE_VSIM" "$SIM_LOG"' EXIT

truncate -s "$SHARED_SIZE" "$SHARED_MEM"

PLUGIN_ARGS="outfile=$STATE_QEMU"
VSIM_TRIG_ARGS=()
if [ "$TRIGGER_MODE" = "pc" ]; then
  PLUGIN_ARGS="$PLUGIN_ARGS,enter_pc=0x$ENTER_PC"
  VSIM_TRIG_ARGS=(--hybrid-exit-pc "0x$EXIT_PC")
fi

echo "[$FIXTURE 1/3 mode=$TRIGGER_MODE] QEMU 1 (shared mmap): drain entry state"
# -cpu andes-ax45mpv,vlen=512: gates Andes-specific CSRs (mcache_ctl,
# mmisc_ctl, ...) and pins VLEN to 512 (vlenb=64) so the V state in the
# drain file matches HYBRID_VLEN_BYTES_MAX. Non-V fixtures run identically
# under either VLEN.
# -semihosting-config enable=on,target=native: lets fixtures print from
# QEMU1 (phase 1) via newlib stdio (linked against libgloss_vh), matching
# the QEMU2 launch in qemu_handback.hpp. Phase 2 (vsim) is forbidden from
# doing any syscall -- vsim does not implement semihosting.
# QEMU1 plugin drains state then exits 200 (== kExitHandoffToQemu) to
# distinguish drain from workload completion (0). See F1 in ROADMAP.md.
set +e
"$VSIM_QEMU" \
  -object "memory-backend-file,id=mem,share=on,mem-path=$SHARED_MEM,size=$SHARED_SIZE" \
  -machine virt,memory-backend=mem \
  -cpu andes-ax45mpv,vlen=512 \
  -bios none -nographic \
  -semihosting-config enable=on,target=native \
  -kernel "$VSIM_ELF" \
  -plugin "$VSIM_PLUGIN,$PLUGIN_ARGS" >/dev/null 2>&1
QEMU1_RC=$?
set -e

if [ "$QEMU1_RC" -ne 200 ]; then
  echo "FAIL[$FIXTURE]: QEMU 1 expected exit 200 on drain, got $QEMU1_RC"
  exit 1
fi

if [ ! -s "$STATE_QEMU" ]; then
  echo "FAIL[$FIXTURE]: QEMU 1 did not produce state"
  exit 1
fi

echo "[$FIXTURE 2/3 mode=$TRIGGER_MODE] vsim (shared mmap): resume + drain + spawn QEMU 2"
set +e
timeout "$TIMEOUT" "$VSIM_BIN" "$VSIM_ELF" \
  --resume-from-qemu "$STATE_QEMU" \
  --handoff-out "$STATE_VSIM" \
  --shared-mem-path "$SHARED_MEM" \
  --shared-mem-size "$SHARED_SIZE" \
  --qemu-binary "$VSIM_QEMU" \
  "${VSIM_TRIG_ARGS[@]}" >"$SIM_LOG" 2>&1
RC=$?
set -e

if [ "$RC" != "$EXPECT_RC" ]; then
  echo "FAIL[$FIXTURE]: expected exit $EXPECT_RC, got $RC"
  echo "--- sim log ---"
  cat "$SIM_LOG"
  exit 1
fi

echo "[$FIXTURE 3/3 mode=$TRIGGER_MODE] PASS: round-trip exit code $RC"

#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Build hybrid_qemu out-of-tree under build/qemu/.
set -euo pipefail

ROOT="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
SRC="$ROOT/hybrid_qemu"
BUILD="$ROOT/build/qemu"

if [[ ! -x "$SRC/configure" ]]; then
  echo "missing $SRC/configure - run 'git submodule update --init --recursive'" >&2
  exit 1
fi

mkdir -p "$BUILD"

if [[ ! -f "$BUILD/build.ninja" && ! -f "$BUILD/Makefile" ]]; then
  (cd "$BUILD" && "$SRC/configure" \
    --target-list=riscv64-softmmu \
    --enable-plugins \
    --disable-werror \
    --disable-libnfs \
    --disable-werror)
fi

make -C "$BUILD" CFLAGS="-Wno-int-conversion -Wno-error=redundant-decls" -j64

-plugin "$VSIM_PLUGIN,$PLUGIN_ARGS" >/dev/null 2>&1
QEMU1_RC=$?
set -e

if [ "$QEMU1_RC" -ne 200 ]; then
  echo "FAIL[$FIXTURE]: QEMU 1 expected exit 200 on drain, got $QEMU1_RC"
  exit 1
fi

if [ ! -s "$STATE_QEMU" ]; then
  echo "FAIL[$FIXTURE]: QEMU 1 did not produce state"
  exit 1
fi

echo "[$FIXTURE 2/3 mode=$TRIGGER_MODE] vsim (shared mmap): resume + drain + spawn QEMU 2"
set +e
timeout "$TIMEOUT" "$VSIM_BIN" "$VSIM_ELF" \
  --resume-from-qemu "$STATE_QEMU" \
  --handoff-out "$STATE_VSIM" \
  --shared-mem-path "$SHARED_MEM" \
  --shared-mem-size "$SHARED_SIZE" \
  --qemu-binary "$VSIM_QEMU" \
  "${VSIM_TRIG_ARGS[@]}" >"$SIM_LOG" 2>&1
RC=$?
set -e

if [ "$RC" != "$EXPECT_RC" ]; then
  echo "FAIL[$FIXTURE]: expected exit $EXPECT_RC, got $RC"
  echo "--- sim log ---"
  cat "$SIM_LOG"
