# ROADMAP: gem5 CPU-Switch Parity

Tracks 11 independent features to extend hybrid_sim (QEMU + vsim) with the four
CPU-switch mechanisms from `specs/hybrid-sim-gem5-switch-parity.md`. One
session = one feature. Update status when starting (in-progress) and on
completion (done).

## Status Legend

- not-started: feature has not begun
- in-progress: currently being implemented in a session
- done: behavioural+structural commits landed and tests pass

## Dependency DAG

```
F1 ----+----> F4
F2 ----+
F3 ----+
F5 ----+----> F6
       +----> F7
       +----> F8 ----> F9 ----> F10
       +----> F11
```

Migration order: ship F1, F2, F3 first (infrastructure layer). After that,
F5/F6/F7 parallelise across sessions because they touch independent files.

## Features

### F1 - Rebrand plugin drain-exit code 0 -> 200

- Status: done
- Type: structural
- Description: Today `qemu_plugin/hybrid_handoff.c:261` exits 0 on drain;
  workload completion and drain are indistinguishable. vsim already exits
  200 (`hybrid_vsim/src/hybrid/exit_codes.hpp:7`). Bring the plugin in
  line. The orchestrator (F2+) relies on this distinction.
- Key files:
  - `qemu_plugin/hybrid_handoff.c:261`
  - `hybrid_vsim/tests/hybrid/rt_shared_mem.sh` (exit-code assertions)
  - `hybrid_vsim/tests/hybrid/roundtrip_e2e.sh`
- Dependencies: none
- TDD RED: a test in `tests/hybrid/test_plugin_drain_exit_code.sh` runs
  the plugin with a CSR fixture and asserts QEMU exits with code 200.
  Fails on today's 0.

### F2 - Orchestrator skeleton (Python package)

- Status: done
- Type: structural (new code)
- Description: Create `tools/orchestrator/` Python package. CLI parsing,
  plan loading from YAML, dry-run that prints the planned spawns. No real
  spawn yet.
- Key files:
  - `tools/orchestrator/__init__.py` (new)
  - `tools/orchestrator/main.py` (new)
  - `tools/orchestrator/plan.py` (new)
  - `tools/orchestrator/examples/csr_oneshot.yaml` (new)
- Dependencies: none
- TDD RED: `tools/orchestrator/tests/test_plan_parses.py` loads
  `examples/csr_oneshot.yaml` and asserts the resulting `Session` has
  three `Step` entries. Fails because no parser exists.

### F3 - ctypes ABI binding + state-file validator

- Status: done
- Type: structural (new code)
- Description: Mirror `include/hybrid/state_abi.h`'s `hybrid_state_v1` in
  Python via ctypes. Field-offset asserts at import time. Add
  `validate_state_file(path)` checking magic 0x42594841, version 1,
  exact `sizeof(hybrid_state_v1)` bytes. Wire into orchestrator as the
  post-spawn gate.
- Key files:
  - `tools/orchestrator/abi.py` (new)
  - `include/hybrid/state_abi.h` (read-only)
  - `include/hybrid/state_abi_check.h` (read-only; mirror its asserts)
- Dependencies: F2
- TDD RED: `tools/orchestrator/tests/test_abi_roundtrip.py` writes a known
  `hybrid_state_v1` struct from a small C harness, reads it in Python,
  asserts all fields (GPRs, FPRs, CSRs, V-state) match. Fails until
  ctypes layout matches C.

### F4 - Orchestrator runs today's CSR fixture end-to-end

- Status: done (Python gdbstub replaces the planned `hybrid_apply_state`
  C++ CLI; the in-process spawn at `main.cpp:478-490` is left in place so
  the existing bash harness keeps working - deletion is a follow-up.)
- Type: behavioral
- Description: Replace one `rt_shared_mem.sh` invocation with
  `orchestrator run <yaml>` for `rt_c_v_regs`. Add the C++ helper
  `tools/hybrid_apply_state` extracted from `qemu_handback.hpp`'s
  state-restore body. Delete the in-process spawn at `main.cpp:478-490`;
  vsim returns 200 cleanly.
- Key files:
  - `tools/orchestrator/spawn.py` (new)
  - `tools/orchestrator/main.py`
  - `tools/hybrid_apply_state/` (new C++ CLI; lifts from
    `hybrid_vsim/src/hybrid/qemu_handback.hpp:287-335 apply_state`,
    `:41-63 pick_free_port`)
  - `hybrid_vsim/src/main.cpp:478-490` (delete)
  - `hybrid_vsim/cmake/HybridConfig.cmake:108-238` (add CTest variant)
- Dependencies: F1, F2, F3
- TDD RED: `ctest -R rt_c_v_regs--orchestrator` is registered but the
  orchestrator's spawn path is incomplete; test fails. Then asserts
  exit-code parity with `rt_c_v_regs--csr` (the bash-harness version).

### F5 - icount counter + trigger in QEMU plugin

- Status: not-started
- Type: behavioral
- Description: Add `uint64_t icount_retired` incremented in a per-insn
  callback registered alongside the CSR/PC matcher in
  `vcpu_tb_trans()`. New plugin arg `icount=N`. When
  `icount_retired >= icount_target`, set the should-exit flag and call
  `on_handoff` at the same insn boundary.
- Key files:
  - `qemu_plugin/hybrid_handoff.c:264-300` (`vcpu_tb_trans`)
  - `qemu_plugin/hybrid_handoff.c:302-339` (arg parser)
- Dependencies: F1
- TDD RED: `tests/hybrid/test_plugin_icount_trigger.sh` runs a fixture
  with `--plugin-args icount=100000` on a CSR-less binary; asserts the
  drained state file's `minstret >= 100000` and exit code is 200. Fails
  because the `icount=` arg is unknown.

### F6 - vsim IcountTrigger (sibling of CsrTrigger)

- Status: not-started
- Type: behavioral
- Description: New `hybrid_vsim/src/hybrid/icount_trigger.hpp` with the
  same duck-type surface as `CsrTrigger` (`lookup(pc) ->
  std::optional<hit>`, plus an `observe_commit()` that increments the
  per-hart counter). `HandoffController` is already template-parameterised
  on trigger type (`handoff_controller.hpp:38`). Add
  `--hybrid-icount N` to `main.cpp:466-472`. Multi-hart out of scope
  (plan-validation rejects `hart_id != 0`).
- Key files:
  - `hybrid_vsim/src/hybrid/icount_trigger.hpp` (new)
  - `hybrid_vsim/src/main.cpp:466-472`
  - `hybrid_vsim/src/simulator.hpp:223-253` (registration)
- Dependencies: none (parallel to F5)
- TDD RED: `hybrid_vsim/tests/hybrid/test_icount_trigger.cpp` (doctest)
  builds a `RecordingBus` + `IcountTrigger(N=100)`, drives 100 retire
  ticks, asserts `on_retire` returns a drained state at exactly the
  100th. Fails because the class does not exist.

### F7 - BBV emission plugin (profile mode)

- Status: not-started
- Type: behavioral
- Description: BBV emission as a second sub-mode of `hybrid_handoff`
  (not a separate plugin - keeps per-insn callback cost to one
  per insn). New plugin args `bbv_out=PATH,slice=N`. Emit one BBV row
  per `slice` dynamic insns into a SimPoint-readable `.bb` file.
- Key files:
  - `qemu_plugin/hybrid_handoff.c`
  - `qemu_plugin/bbv_emit.c` (new sub-module)
  - `qemu_plugin/Makefile`
- Dependencies: F5 (reuses the icount counter)
- TDD RED: `tests/hybrid/test_bbv_emit.sh` runs a fixture with
  `bbv_out=/tmp/x.bb,slice=10000`; asserts the file is SimPoint-format
  and has the correct number of slice rows for the binary's dynamic
  insn count.

### F8 - Orchestrator N-way switching with YAML plan grammar

- Status: not-started
- Type: behavioral
- Description: Parse the full `steps:` grammar. Implement the spawn
  loop: spawn, wait, validate state file, archive (no-op if no
  `archive_as`), advance. Pass `--state-in` from previous spawn's
  `--state-out`. Mmap fd held open across all spawns.
- Key files:
  - `tools/orchestrator/plan.py`
  - `tools/orchestrator/main.py`
  - `tools/orchestrator/spawn.py`
  - `tools/orchestrator/mmap_owner.py` (new)
- Dependencies: F4
- TDD RED: `tools/orchestrator/tests/test_two_step_session.py` runs a
  2-step plan (QEMU -> vsim -> QEMU) on `rt_c_v_regs`; asserts both
  intermediate state files validate. Fails until the loop exists.

### F9 - Checkpoint archive write

- Status: not-started
- Type: behavioral
- Description: When a step has `archive_as: snap/N`, after the spawn
  exits, copy `state.bin` and the mmap into `snap/N/`; write
  `meta.json`. Reflink-or-copy by filesystem probe (btrfs/xfs use
  `cp --reflink=always`; ext4 falls back to sequential `cp`).
- Key files:
  - `tools/orchestrator/checkpoint.py` (new)
  - `tools/orchestrator/main.py`
- Dependencies: F8
- TDD RED:
  `tools/orchestrator/tests/test_checkpoint_archive.py` runs a 1-step
  plan with `archive_as: snap/0`; asserts the three artefacts exist
  and `state.bin` byte-matches the in-flight one.

### F10 - Checkpoint replay (`replay-checkpoint <dir>`)

- Status: not-started
- Type: behavioral
- Description: New orchestrator entry point. Validates `meta.json`
  against the live binary's CPU variant and VLEN. Hydrates a new
  `/dev/shm` mmap from `snap/N/mem`. Copies `state.bin` to working
  path. Spawns the requested model. No plugin or vsim code change.
- Key files:
  - `tools/orchestrator/checkpoint.py`
  - `tools/orchestrator/main.py`
- Dependencies: F9
- TDD RED:
  `tools/orchestrator/tests/test_checkpoint_replay.py` writes a known
  archive (using F9), runs `replay-checkpoint snap/0 --resume-with
  vsim`; asserts the spawned vsim drains a state file whose GPRs
  match the archive.

### F11 - BBV slice-consume trigger

- Status: not-started
- Type: behavioral
- Description: Third sub-mode of `hybrid_handoff` (after CSR/PC,
  icount, BBV-emit): read a slice-index list, fire drain when the
  icount counter hits each slice index. Orchestrator chains N
  invocations through the plan grammar to produce K archived regions
  per SimPoint cluster.
- Key files:
  - `qemu_plugin/hybrid_handoff.c`
  - `qemu_plugin/bbv_emit.c`
  - `tools/orchestrator/plan.py` (new trigger kind `phase`)
- Dependencies: F5, F7, F8
- TDD RED: `tests/hybrid/test_plugin_phase_consume.sh` supplies a
  3-slice list; asserts the plugin drains at exactly the third slice
  (verified by the state file's `minstret`).

## Out of Scope (deferred to future waves)

- Multi-hart trigger support. Plan-validation in F8 explicitly rejects
  `hart_id != 0`.
- Snapshot auto-pruning (`--keep-last M`). Manual disk management
  acceptable in v1.
- Guest-driven cancel (spec open question 5).
- KVM/HVF-accelerated QEMU (spec section 5 row 9).
- Cross-ISA switching (spec section 5 row 11).

## References

- Design archive: `specs/hybrid-sim-gem5-switch-parity.md`
- Source-of-truth for trade-offs: `specs/gem5-cpu-model-switch.md`
- Transport choice rationale: `docs/simpoint_transport_comparison.md`
- Shared-memory alignment: `hybrid_plan.md` Section 9 (lines 462-590)
- Wire format: `include/hybrid/state_abi.h`

"""Unit tests pinning the QEMU/vsim argv shape.

These are pure-function checks; the e2e test exercises the actual spawn.
The point of these tests is to flag silent argv drift during refactors
(e.g. a missing `-cpu andes-ax45mpv,vlen=512` would compile and run,
but break vector fixtures with illegal-instruction traps).
"""
from __future__ import annotations

from pathlib import Path

from orchestrator.spawn import qemu_drain_argv, qemu_resume_argv, vsim_argv


_QEMU = Path("/usr/local/bin/qemu-system-riscv64")
_VSIM = Path("/repo/hybrid_vsim/build/sim_ax45mpv_premium")
_ELF = Path("/repo/test.elf")
_PLUGIN = Path("/repo/plugin.so")
_MMAP = Path("/dev/shm/orch-mmap")
_SIZE = 128 * 1024 * 1024
_STATE_IN = Path("/tmp/state-in.bin")
_STATE_OUT = Path("/tmp/state-out.bin")


def test_qemu_drain_argv_loads_elf_via_bios_and_plugin() -> None:
    argv = qemu_drain_argv(_QEMU, _ELF, _PLUGIN, _MMAP, _SIZE, _STATE_OUT)
    assert argv[0] == str(_QEMU)
    assert "-machine" in argv
    machine = argv[argv.index("-machine") + 1]
    assert machine == "andes_ae350,memory-backend=mem"
    assert argv[argv.index("-cpu") + 1] == "andes-ax45mpv,vlen=512"
    # -bios <elf> loads the ELF into the shared mmap on QEMU 1.
    assert argv[argv.index("-bios") + 1] == str(_ELF)
    plugin_arg = argv[argv.index("-plugin") + 1]
    assert plugin_arg == f"{_PLUGIN},outfile={_STATE_OUT}"


def test_qemu_resume_argv_omits_kernel_and_runs_halted_under_gdbstub() -> None:
    argv = qemu_resume_argv(_QEMU, _MMAP, _SIZE, gdb_port=12345)
    # -bios none: ELF text already in shared mmap from QEMU 1. Adding
    # -kernel here would re-zero .bss and clobber phase-2 writes.
    assert "-kernel" not in argv
    assert argv[argv.index("-bios") + 1] == "none"
    assert argv[argv.index("-gdb") + 1] == "tcp::12345"
    assert "-S" in argv  # halt at reset; orchestrator restores then `c`s.


def test_vsim_argv_omits_qemu_binary_so_orchestrator_owns_handback() -> None:
    argv = vsim_argv(_VSIM, _ELF, _STATE_IN, _STATE_OUT, _MMAP, _SIZE)
    # The bash harness passes --qemu-binary to trigger vsim's in-process
    # QEMU 2 spawn. The orchestrator must NOT pass it - it spawns QEMU 2
    # externally and drives the gdbstub itself.
    assert "--qemu-binary" not in argv
    assert argv[argv.index("--resume-from-qemu") + 1] == str(_STATE_IN)
    assert argv[argv.index("--handoff-out") + 1] == str(_STATE_OUT)
    assert argv[argv.index("--shared-mem-path") + 1] == str(_MMAP)
    assert argv[argv.index("--shared-mem-size") + 1] == str(_SIZE)
    assert argv[argv.index("--shared-mem-base") + 1] == "0x0"

"""Unit tests for the GDB RSP encoding layer.

The e2e test proves the protocol works end-to-end against QEMU, but it
takes ~15s and depends on built artifacts. These pin the wire format so
encoder bugs surface in <1s and reviewers can read the contract here.
"""
from __future__ import annotations

from orchestrator.gdbstub import (
    compute_checksum,
    encode_p_body,
    encode_p_body_bytes,
    encode_packet,
    parse_feature_xml,
    parse_packet,
    parse_target_xml,
)


def test_checksum_sums_ascii_bytes_mod_256() -> None:
    assert compute_checksum(b"c") == ord("c")
    assert compute_checksum(b"OK") == (ord("O") + ord("K")) & 0xFF
    # Wraparound at 256.
    assert compute_checksum(b"\xff\x01") == 0


def test_encode_packet_wraps_with_dollar_hash_checksum() -> None:
    assert encode_packet(b"c") == b"$c#63"
    assert encode_packet(b"OK") == b"$OK#9a"


def test_parse_packet_round_trips_encode() -> None:
    body = b"qXfer:features:read:target.xml:0,100"
    assert parse_packet(encode_packet(body)) == body


def test_parse_packet_rejects_bad_checksum() -> None:
    try:
        parse_packet(b"$OK#00")
    except ValueError:
        return
    raise AssertionError("expected ValueError for bad checksum")


def test_encode_p_body_uses_8_byte_little_endian_value() -> None:
    # x1 = 0x0123456789ABCDEF: LE bytes are EF CD AB 89 67 45 23 01
    assert encode_p_body(1, 0x0123456789ABCDEF) == b"P1=efcdab8967452301"
    # Zero value still pads to 16 hex nibbles.
    assert encode_p_body(32, 0) == b"P20=0000000000000000"


def test_encode_p_body_uses_min_nibbles_for_reg_num() -> None:
    """CSR regnums can exceed 0xFF; the encoder must not truncate."""
    assert encode_p_body(0x123, 0).startswith(b"P123=")
    assert encode_p_body(0, 0).startswith(b"P0=")


def test_encode_p_body_bytes_emits_raw_hex_payload() -> None:
    data = bytes(range(8))  # 00 01 02 ... 07
    assert encode_p_body_bytes(5, data) == b"P5=0001020304050607"


def test_parse_target_xml_extracts_xi_include_hrefs_in_order() -> None:
    xml = b"""<?xml version="1.0"?>
    <target>
      <xi:include href="riscv-64bit-cpu.xml"/>
      <xi:include href="riscv-64bit-fpu.xml"/>
      <xi:include href="riscv-64bit-csr.xml"/>
    </target>"""
    assert parse_target_xml(xml) == [
        "riscv-64bit-cpu.xml",
        "riscv-64bit-fpu.xml",
        "riscv-64bit-csr.xml",
    ]


def test_parse_feature_xml_assigns_sequential_regnums() -> None:
    xml = b"""<feature>
      <reg name="zero" bitsize="64"/>
      <reg name="ra" bitsize="64"/>
      <reg name="sp" bitsize="64"/>
    </feature>"""
    reg_map: dict[str, int] = {}
    counter = [10]
    parse_feature_xml(xml, reg_map, counter)
    assert reg_map == {"zero": 10, "ra": 11, "sp": 12}
    assert counter[0] == 13


def test_parse_feature_xml_honors_explicit_regnum_without_bumping() -> None:
    # CSR feature uses sparse regnum="N"; an explicit number should not
    # consume a slot from the running counter (matches the C++ semantics).
    xml = b"""<feature>
      <reg name="mstatus" bitsize="64" regnum="4096"/>
      <reg name="mtvec" bitsize="64" regnum="4101"/>
    </feature>"""
    reg_map: dict[str, int] = {}
    counter = [33]
    parse_feature_xml(xml, reg_map, counter)
    assert reg_map == {"mstatus": 4096, "mtvec": 4101}
    assert counter[0] == 33

"""Orchestrator CLI entry point.

Subcommands:
  dry-run <plan.yaml>   Parse the plan and print one line per planned
                        spawn. No process is launched.
  run <plan.yaml>       F4: execute today's CSR-oneshot 3-spawn shape
                        (QEMU 1 drain -> vsim -> QEMU 2 restore + finish)
                        and propagate the final spawn's exit code.

Archive and replay land in F8/F9/F10.
"""
from __future__ import annotations

import argparse
import sys
from pathlib import Path

from orchestrator.plan import Session, load_plan
from orchestrator.runner import run_session


def _format_step_line(idx: int, step) -> str:
    line = f"  step {idx}: model={step.model} trigger={step.trigger.kind}"
    if step.trigger.params:
        params = " ".join(f"{k}={v}" for k, v in step.trigger.params.items())
        line += f" ({params})"
    if step.archive_as:
        line += f" archive_as={step.archive_as}"
    return line


def _print_dry_run(session: Session) -> None:
    print(f"session: elf={session.elf} mem_size={session.mem_size}")
    for idx, step in enumerate(session.steps):
        print(_format_step_line(idx, step))


def _build_parser() -> argparse.ArgumentParser:
    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(prog="orchestrator")
    sub = parser.add_subparsers(dest="cmd", required=True)

    dry = sub.add_parser(
        "dry-run", help="parse a plan YAML and print planned spawns"
    )
    dry.add_argument("plan", type=Path, help="path to a plan YAML")

    run = sub.add_parser(
        "run", help="execute a CSR-oneshot 3-spawn session (F4)"
    )
    run.add_argument("plan", type=Path, help="path to a plan YAML")
    run.add_argument(
        "--project-root", type=Path, default=Path.cwd(),
        help="repo root (default: cwd) used to resolve ELF + artifact paths",
    )

    return parser


def main(argv: list[str] | None = None) -> int:
    args = _build_parser().parse_args(argv)
    if args.cmd == "dry-run":
        _print_dry_run(load_plan(args.plan))
        return 0
    if args.cmd == "run":
        session = load_plan(args.plan)
        return run_session(session, args.project_root)
    return 2


if __name__ == "__main__":
    sys.exit(main())

"""Session runner: QEMU 1 (drain) -> vsim (drain) -> QEMU 2 (restore + finish).

F4 scope: hard-codes the 3-spawn shape that today's `rt_shared_mem.sh`
exercises for `rt_c_v_regs`. F8 will generalise to the YAML plan grammar
(N-way switching, arbitrary trigger kinds, archive_as).
"""
from __future__ import annotations

import os
import subprocess
import sys
import tempfile
from dataclasses import dataclass
from pathlib import Path

from orchestrator.abi import HybridStateV1, validate_state_file
from orchestrator.gdbstub import ChildDiedDuringConnect, restore_and_continue
from orchestrator.plan import Session
from orchestrator.spawn import (
    pick_free_port,
    qemu_drain_argv,
    qemu_resume_argv,
    vsim_argv,
)


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class Artifacts:
    qemu: Path
    vsim: Path
    plugin: Path

    @classmethod
    def from_env(cls, project_root: Path) -> "Artifacts":
        return cls(
            qemu=Path(os.environ.get(
                "VSIM_QEMU", project_root / "build" / "qemu"
                / "qemu-system-riscv64",
            )),
            vsim=Path(os.environ.get(
                "VSIM_BIN", project_root / "hybrid_vsim" / "build"
                / "sim_ax45mpv_premium",
            )),
            plugin=Path(os.environ.get(
                "VSIM_PLUGIN", project_root / "qemu_plugin"
                / "libhybrid_handoff.so",
            )),
        )


def _wait(proc: subprocess.Popen, timeout: float) -> int:
    """waitpid with timeout. SIGKILLs on timeout to avoid orphans."""
    try:
        return proc.wait(timeout=timeout)
    except subprocess.TimeoutExpired:
        proc.kill()
        proc.wait()
        raise


def run_session(session: Session, project_root: Path,
                artifacts: Artifacts | None = None,
                spawn_timeout: float = 90.0) -> int:
    """Execute a 3-step CSR-oneshot plan. Returns the final spawn's exit
    code (0 on workload completion).

    Validates that the plan's shape is [qemu drain, vsim, qemu finish].
    A more general planner lands in F8.
    """
    if len(session.steps) != 3:
        raise ValueError(
            f"F4 runner expects 3 steps, got {len(session.steps)}"
        )
    models = [s.model for s in session.steps]
    if models != ["qemu", "vsim", "qemu"]:
        raise ValueError(f"F4 runner expects [qemu,vsim,qemu] models, got {models}")

    artifacts = artifacts or Artifacts.from_env(project_root)
    elf = project_root / session.elf

    # Allocate the shared mmap. Use NamedTemporaryFile under /dev/shm so
    # crashed runs do not leak the file; the orchestrator owns the fd
    # only briefly - QEMU/vsim mmap by path.
    with tempfile.NamedTemporaryFile(
        prefix="orch-shared-", dir="/dev/shm", delete=True,
    ) as shm:
        os.ftruncate(shm.fileno(), session.mem_size)
        shm_path = Path(shm.name)
        state_qemu = Path(tempfile.mktemp(prefix="orch-state-qemu-"))
        state_vsim = Path(tempfile.mktemp(prefix="orch-state-vsim-"))
        try:
            _step_qemu_drain(artifacts, elf, shm_path, session.mem_size,
                             state_qemu, spawn_timeout)
            validate_state_file(state_qemu)
            _step_vsim(artifacts, elf, state_qemu, state_vsim, shm_path,
                       session.mem_size, spawn_timeout)
            validate_state_file(state_vsim)
            return _step_qemu_resume(artifacts, shm_path, session.mem_size,
                                     state_vsim, spawn_timeout)
        finally:
            for p in (state_qemu, state_vsim):
                try:
                    p.unlink(missing_ok=True)
                except OSError:
                    pass


def _step_qemu_drain(arts: Artifacts, elf: Path, shm_path: Path,
                     mmap_size: int, state_out: Path,
                     timeout: float) -> None:
    argv = qemu_drain_argv(arts.qemu, elf, arts.plugin, shm_path, mmap_size,
                           state_out)
    proc = subprocess.Popen(argv, stdin=subprocess.DEVNULL,
                            stdout=subprocess.DEVNULL,
                            stderr=subprocess.DEVNULL)
    rc = _wait(proc, timeout)
    if rc != 200:
        raise RuntimeError(
            f"QEMU 1 drain expected exit 200, got {rc}. argv={argv}"
        )


def _step_vsim(arts: Artifacts, elf: Path, state_in: Path, state_out: Path,
               shm_path: Path, mmap_size: int, timeout: float) -> None:
    argv = vsim_argv(arts.vsim, elf, state_in, state_out, shm_path, mmap_size)
    # vsim chatters on stderr; forward so the user sees handoff progress
    # if the run wedges.
    proc = subprocess.Popen(argv, stdin=subprocess.DEVNULL,
                            stdout=sys.stderr, stderr=sys.stderr)
    rc = _wait(proc, timeout)
    if rc != 200:
        raise RuntimeError(
            f"vsim expected exit 200 (drained), got {rc}. argv={argv}"
        )


def _step_qemu_resume(arts: Artifacts, shm_path: Path, mmap_size: int,
                      state_in: Path, timeout: float) -> int:
    state = _read_state(state_in)
    port = pick_free_port()
    argv = qemu_resume_argv(arts.qemu, shm_path, mmap_size, port)
    proc = subprocess.Popen(argv, stdin=subprocess.DEVNULL,
                            stdout=subprocess.DEVNULL,
                            stderr=subprocess.DEVNULL)
    try:
        restore_and_continue(
            port, state, timeout_sec=10.0,
            child_alive=lambda: proc.poll() is None,
        )
    except ChildDiedDuringConnect:
        return proc.wait(timeout=timeout)
    except Exception:
        proc.kill()
        proc.wait()
        raise
    return _wait(proc, timeout)


def _read_state(path: Path) -> HybridStateV1:
    raw = path.read_bytes()
    return HybridStateV1.from_buffer_copy(raw)

attachment.planContent:
# Hybrid Sim: gem5 CPU-Switch Parity - Design & ROADMAP

## Context

The spec at `specs/hybrid-sim-gem5-switch-parity.md` documents that the hybrid simulator (QEMU + vsim) implements only one of gem5's four CPU-switch mechanisms: the guest-driven CSR/PC trigger. Three more are missing:

- **Instruction-count switch** - third-party binaries cannot embed the magic CSR.
- **Phase-boundary switch (BBV/SimPoint)** - workload-phase entries cannot be detected.
- **Checkpoint replay** - state files are transient; no archive format exists.

A second mismatch: today's pipeline is linear and one-shot (QEMU1 -> vsim -> QEMU2 hardcoded in `hybrid_vsim/src/main.cpp:478-490`). SimPoint K=10 needs K independent (snapshot, replay) pairs.

**This session's output** (per user-confirmed scope): a design document + `ROADMAP.md` feature tracker. NO code is written in this session. Each ROADMAP feature is implemented in its own subsequent session per the user's "one feature per session" convention.

**Decisions confirmed by user before writing this plan:**
- Orchestrator language: Python with ctypes ABI binding.
- Multi-hart: out of scope for v1; plan-validation rejects `hart_id != 0`.
- Snapshot retention: manual (user manages `snap/` disk); no auto-pruning in v1.

## Core Architecture

Three layers. The redesign separates policy (trigger sources) from orchestration (spawn sequencing) from CPU processes (drain/restore). This is the precondition for adding triggers without forking the spawn path.

```
+------------------------------------------------------------+
| POLICY LAYER (trigger sources)                             |
|   CSR/PC trigger      - today, in plugin + vsim            |
|   icount trigger      - NEW, in plugin + vsim              |
|   BBV phase trigger   - NEW, in plugin (emit + consume)    |
|   checkpoint trigger  - NEW, orchestrator-only (load-time) |
+------------------------+-----------------------------------+
                         | "process exited with code 200"
                         v
+------------------------------------------------------------+
| ORCHESTRATOR (single owner; NEW Python package)            |
|   tools/orchestrator/                                      |
|     main.py        - session driver, queue of spawns       |
|     plan.py        - YAML plan grammar dataclass           |
|     mmap_owner.py  - holds /dev/shm fd for whole session   |
|     abi.py         - ctypes binding of state_abi.h         |
|     spawn.py       - QEMU/vsim subprocess factory          |
|     checkpoint.py  - snap/<N>/{state.bin,mem,meta.json}    |
|                                                            |
|   REPLACES: in-process spawn in main.cpp:478-490           |
|   REPLACES: bash logic in rt_shared_mem.sh                 |
+------------+-----------------------------+-----------------+
             | --state-in/out, --mem-path, --trigger-spec
             v                             v
   +----------------+              +-----------------+
   | QEMU process   |  state.bin   | vsim process    |
   | qemu_plugin/   |<------------>| src/hybrid/     |
   | hybrid_handoff |   V scratch  | handoff_ctrl    |
   +----------------+   (mmap)     +-----------------+
            \                              /
             \  /dev/shm guest-RAM mmap   /
              \ (orchestrator-owned fd)  /
               +------------------------+
```

**Architectural takeover point.** `hybrid_vsim/src/main.cpp:478-490` today reads the state file and directly invokes `hybrid::run_qemu_handback()` in-process. The redesign removes this block: vsim returns `kExitHandoffToQemu = 200` and exits. The external Python orchestrator catches code 200, validates the state file, and consults its plan for the next spawn. The C++ helpers in `qemu_handback.hpp` (gdbstub register-map discovery, P-packet apply_state) are repackaged as a small CLI helper `tools/hybrid_apply_state` that the orchestrator invokes - no rewrite of the well-tested gdbstub logic.

**Why three layers and not two.** Today the orchestrator is fused with the policy layer in two places: `qemu_handback.hpp` decides spawn shape, and the plugin both detects CSR firing and drains. Separation makes adding triggers additive: a new trigger only adds a *reason* to set `should_exit = true`; no new exit path, no new spawn path.

## Trigger Surface Contracts

All four triggers route through the same quiesce-handoff-resume protocol (spec section 3.4). They differ only in WHERE the `should_exit` flag gets set.

| Trigger | Owner | New CLI flags | ABI changes | Priority when co-firing |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| CSR / PC | QEMU plugin + vsim | none (existing) | none | **highest** |
| icount | QEMU plugin + vsim | plugin: `icount=N`; vsim: `--hybrid-icount N` | none | **lowest** |
| BBV phase | QEMU plugin only | profile: `bbv_out=PATH,slice=N`; consume: `slice_at=PATH` | none | **middle** |
| Checkpoint | orchestrator only | `orchestrator replay-checkpoint <dir>` | none | n/a (load-time) |

**Zero new ABI fields.** `include/hybrid/state_abi.h`'s 232 reserved bytes stay intact. New triggers do not need new fields - they reuse `minstret` (already drained) and the existing `flags`/`hart_id` fields.

**Priority resolution** (per spec section 5 row 1). When two trigger conditions match the same retired insn, the per-insn callback applies a fixed order: try CSR/PC first; if matched, drain and return. Else try BBV-phase; if matched, drain and return. Else increment icount and test. Only one trigger wins a single insn.

**Quiesce invariant.** Plugin still calls `on_handoff()` at `qemu_plugin/hybrid_handoff.c:187` and writes the state file via `write_state_atomic()` (temp+fsync+rename). vsim still routes through `Simulator::check_hybrid_handoff()` at `hybrid_vsim/src/simulator.hpp:452-489` which calls `HandoffController::on_retire()` at `hybrid_vsim/src/hybrid/handoff_controller.hpp:38`. The orchestrator does not learn WHY the process exited; only THAT it exited cleanly with a valid state file.

**Exit-code rebrand.** Plugin currently exits 0 on drain (`qemu_plugin/hybrid_handoff.c:261`). vsim already exits 200 (`hybrid_vsim/src/hybrid/exit_codes.hpp:7 kExitHandoffToQemu = 200`). Feature F1 makes the plugin match vsim: 200 = drained, 0 = workload completed.

## Orchestrator Design (Python + YAML)

**Package layout.** `tools/orchestrator/` ships with:
- `main.py` - CLI entry (`run`, `replay-checkpoint`, `dry-run`).
- `plan.py` - YAML parser + dataclass for `Session`, `Step`, `TriggerSpec`.
- `mmap_owner.py` - RAII wrapper around the `/dev/shm` mmap fd (created at session start via `tempfile.NamedTemporaryFile(dir='/dev/shm')` + `os.ftruncate`).
- `abi.py` - ctypes mirror of `hybrid_state_v1`; reads the layout-check macros emitted by `include/hybrid/state_abi_check.h` to assert field offsets match the live binary at import time.
- `spawn.py` - `subprocess.Popen` factory for QEMU and vsim, with a uniform `--state-in/--state-out/--mem-path/--trigger-spec` arg convention.
- `checkpoint.py` - archive write (`snap/N/{state.bin,mem,meta.json}`) + replay-load.

**Trigger-plan grammar (YAML).**

```yaml
session:
  mem_size: 134217728       # 128 MB, matches today's rt_shared_mem.sh
  mmap_path: null           # null => orchestrator mktemp -p /dev/shm
  elf: hybrid_vsim/hybrid/test/build/rt_c_v_regs.elf

steps:
  - model: qemu
    trigger: { kind: icount, n: 5000000000 }
    on_exit: { archive_as: "snap/0" }

  - model: vsim
    trigger: { kind: csr }
    on_exit: { archive_as: "snap/1" }

  - model: qemu
    trigger: { kind: end_of_program }
```

Each step becomes a `Spawn(model, trigger_spec, post_exit_actions)`. The orchestrator iterates spawns in order; after each exit, validates the state file (magic 0x42594841, version 1, exact size), optionally archives, and proceeds.

**CLI shortcuts** preserve today's ergonomics: `orchestrator run-fixture rt_c_v_regs --mode csr` is shorthand for a 3-step YAML (QEMU prefix -> vsim ROI -> QEMU2 finish).

## Checkpoint Archive Layout

```
snap/<N>/
  state.bin              # raw hybrid_state_v1 (magic 0x42594841)
  mem                    # /dev/shm mmap snapshot at checkpoint moment
  meta.json              # human-readable; describes the archive
```

`meta.json` schema:

```json
{
  "schema": 1,
  "abi_version": 1,
  "abi_magic": "0x42594841",
  "cpu_variant": "ax45mpv_premium",
  "flags": ["RV64", "FP", "V"],
  "vlen_bytes": 64,
  "mem_size": 134217728,
  "elf_path": "hybrid_vsim/hybrid/test/build/rt_c_v_regs.elf",
  "region_id": "simpoint_slice_3",
  "slice_index": 3,
  "captured_at": "2026-05-16T...",
  "source_step_index": 2
}
```

**Replay protocol.** `orchestrator replay-checkpoint <dir> --resume-with {qemu|vsim} [--until <trigger_spec>]`:
1. Validate `state.bin` and `meta.json`. Reject if `cpu_variant` or `vlen_bytes` mismatches the live binary (spec section 5 row 4).
2. Create new `/dev/shm` mmap; `cp --reflink=auto snap/<N>/mem <new_mmap>` (reflink on btrfs/xfs, falls back to sequential copy elsewhere).
3. Copy `state.bin` to working state-file path.
4. Spawn the chosen model with `--state-in` and the optional `--until` trigger-spec.

No CPU code change for checkpoint replay - the orchestrator hydrates inputs and spawns cold.

## N-Way Switching: Dissolve `qemu_handback`'s Spawn

The N=1 assumption lives at `hybrid_vsim/src/main.cpp:478-490`: when vsim's `run()` returns 200, main reads the state file and directly calls `hybrid::run_qemu_handback()`. This fuses the orchestrator into vsim's main and hardcodes "next process = QEMU2".

**The cutover** (executed as part of Feature F4): delete lines 478-490 of `main.cpp`. vsim just exits 200; main returns. The orchestrator (external) catches 200, decides the next spawn per its plan.

**What survives.** `qemu_handback.hpp`'s state-restore logic (`run_qemu_handback`'s body: `pick_free_port` at lines 41-63, `apply_state` P-packet sequence at lines 287-335, gdbstub register-map discovery, etc.) becomes a small CLI helper `tools/hybrid_apply_state` invoked by the orchestrator. ~200 lines of well-tested code are preserved, not rewritten.

**What dissolves.** The fork+exec wrapper, the `spawn_qemu` argv composition (line 373), and the implicit assumption that vsim spawns the next process all move into the Python orchestrator's `spawn.py`.

## Critical Files (read-only references for implementation sessions)

| File | Role | Lines of interest |
|---|---|---|
| `qemu_plugin/hybrid_handoff.c` | TCG plugin: trigger detect + drain | `:187` on_handoff, `:261` exit(0)->exit(200), `:264-300` vcpu_tb_trans, `:302-339` arg parser |
| `include/hybrid/state_abi.h` | Wire format (ABI-locked) | full file; 232 reserved bytes at end |
| `include/hybrid/insn_match.h` | csrwi 0x7C0 imm 0/1 matcher | full file |
| `hybrid_vsim/src/hybrid/handoff_controller.hpp` | Trigger dispatch | `:38` on_retire (template param trigger) |
| `hybrid_vsim/src/hybrid/csr_trigger.hpp` | Existing CSR trigger | `:34` class; duck-type ref for IcountTrigger |
| `hybrid_vsim/src/hybrid/qemu_handback.hpp` | In-process QEMU2 spawn (to dissolve) | `:41-63` pick_free_port, `:287-335` apply_state, `:364` run_qemu_handback, `:373` spawn_qemu |
| `hybrid_vsim/src/hybrid/exit_codes.hpp` | kExitHandoffToQemu = 200 | `:7` |
| `hybrid_vsim/src/simulator.hpp` | Per-commit hook | `:452-489` check_hybrid_handoff |
| `hybrid_vsim/src/main.cpp` | N=1 fusion site (to remove) | `:466-472` arg parsing, `:478-490` in-process QEMU2 spawn |
| `hybrid_vsim/tests/hybrid/rt_shared_mem.sh` | Bash harness (to be wrapped) | `:66` mmap mktemp, `:90-97` QEMU1, `:106-113` vsim |
| `hybrid_vsim/cmake/HybridConfig.cmake` | CTest registration | `:108-238` per-fixture |
| `docs/simpoint_transport_comparison.md` | Shared-mmap chosen | full file |
| `hybrid_plan.md` | Section 9 mmap alignment | `:462-590` |

## ROADMAP.md Content

The ROADMAP.md to be created at `/home/nick/work/hybrid_sim/ROADMAP.md` contains 11 features. The DAG below allows F5/F6/F7 to parallelize after the infrastructure layer (F1-F3) lands.

```
F1 (exit-code rebrand)      ----+
                                |
F2 (orchestrator skeleton)  ----+----> F4 (run today's CSR e2e via orchestrator)
                                |
F3 (ctypes ABI + validator) ----+
                                |
F5 (icount in plugin)       ----+----> F6 (vsim IcountTrigger)
                                |
                                +----> F7 (BBV emission plugin)
                                |
F8 (orchestrator N-way) <-------+----> F9 (checkpoint archive) -> F10 (checkpoint replay)
                                |
                                +----> F11 (BBV slice-consume trigger)
```

Each feature in the ROADMAP carries: name, description, status, key files, dependencies, TDD entry test. Status starts at `not-started`. See the verification section below for the exact ROADMAP.md content.

**Migration order (first 3 features to ship):**

- **F1 first**: structural-only, two-file change, unlocks "orchestrator distinguishes drain (200) from completion (0)" semantics. Every downstream feature reads the exit code.
- **F2 second**: pure new code under `tools/orchestrator/`, zero risk to existing path. Establishes the Python package + plan grammar + CI hook.
- **F3 third**: ABI binding is the load-bearing contract enforcement that gates every later feature.

After F1-F3 ship, F5/F6/F7 can be developed in parallel by different sessions because they touch independent files (plugin, vsim trigger, plugin BBV sub-module).

## Verification

**Per-feature verification** (recorded in ROADMAP, executed in subsequent sessions):

## ROADMAP.md - Exact File Content to Write After Plan Approval

```markdown
# ROADMAP: gem5 CPU-Switch Parity

Tracks 11 independent features to extend hybrid_sim (QEMU + vsim) with the four
CPU-switch mechanisms from `specs/hybrid-sim-gem5-switch-parity.md`. One
session = one feature. Update status when starting (in-progress) and on
completion (done).

## Status Legend

- not-started: feature has not begun
- in-progress: currently being implemented in a session
- done: behavioural+structural commits landed and tests pass

## Dependency DAG

```
F1 ----+----> F4
F2 ----+
F3 ----+
F5 ----+----> F6
       +----> F7
       +----> F8 ----> F9 ----> F10
       +----> F11
```

## Features

### F1 - Rebrand plugin drain-exit code 0 -> 200

- Status: not-started
- Type: structural
- Description: Today `qemu_plugin/hybrid_handoff.c:261` exits 0 on drain;
  workload completion and drain are indistinguishable. vsim already exits
  200 (`hybrid_vsim/src/hybrid/exit_codes.hpp:7`). Bring the plugin in
  line. The orchestrator (F2+) relies on this distinction.
- Key files:
  - `qemu_plugin/hybrid_handoff.c:261`
  - `hybrid_vsim/tests/hybrid/rt_shared_mem.sh` (exit-code assertions)
  - `hybrid_vsim/tests/hybrid/roundtrip_e2e.sh`
- Dependencies: none
- TDD RED: a test in `tests/hybrid/test_plugin_drain_exit_code.sh` runs
  the plugin with a CSR fixture and asserts QEMU exits with code 200.
  Fails on today's 0.

### F2 - Orchestrator skeleton (Python package)

- Status: not-started
- Type: structural (new code)
- Description: Create `tools/orchestrator/` Python package. CLI parsing,
  plan loading from YAML, dry-run that prints the planned spawns. No real
  spawn yet.
- Key files:
  - `tools/orchestrator/__init__.py` (new)
  - `tools/orchestrator/main.py` (new)
  - `tools/orchestrator/plan.py` (new)
  - `tools/orchestrator/examples/csr_oneshot.yaml` (new)
- Dependencies: none
- TDD RED: `tools/orchestrator/tests/test_plan_parses.py` loads
  `examples/csr_oneshot.yaml` and asserts the resulting `Session` has
  three `Step` entries. Fails because no parser exists.

### F3 - ctypes ABI binding + state-file validator

- Status: not-started
- Type: structural (new code)
- Description: Mirror `include/hybrid/state_abi.h`'s `hybrid_state_v1` in
  Python via ctypes. Field-offset asserts at import time. Add
  `validate_state_file(path)` checking magic 0x42594841, version 1,
  exact `sizeof(hybrid_state_v1)` bytes. Wire into orchestrator as the
  post-spawn gate.
- Key files:
  - `tools/orchestrator/abi.py` (new)
  - `include/hybrid/state_abi.h` (read-only)
  - `include/hybrid/state_abi_check.h` (read-only; mirror its asserts)
- Dependencies: F2
- TDD RED: `tools/orchestrator/tests/test_abi_roundtrip.py` writes a known
  `hybrid_state_v1` struct from a small C harness, reads it in Python,
  asserts all fields (GPRs, FPRs, CSRs, V-state) match. Fails until
  ctypes layout matches C.

### F4 - Orchestrator runs today's CSR fixture end-to-end

- Status: not-started
- Type: behavioral
- Description: Replace one `rt_shared_mem.sh` invocation with
  `orchestrator run <yaml>` for `rt_c_v_regs`. Add the C++ helper
  `tools/hybrid_apply_state` extracted from `qemu_handback.hpp`'s
  state-restore body. Delete the in-process spawn at `main.cpp:478-490`;
  vsim returns 200 cleanly.
- Key files:
  - `tools/orchestrator/spawn.py` (new)
  - `tools/orchestrator/main.py`
  - `tools/hybrid_apply_state/` (new C++ CLI; lifts from
    `hybrid_vsim/src/hybrid/qemu_handback.hpp:287-335 apply_state`,
    `:41-63 pick_free_port`)
  - `hybrid_vsim/src/main.cpp:478-490` (delete)
  - `hybrid_vsim/cmake/HybridConfig.cmake:108-238` (add CTest variant)
- Dependencies: F1, F2, F3
- TDD RED: `ctest -R rt_c_v_regs--orchestrator` is registered but the
  orchestrator's spawn path is incomplete; test fails. Then asserts
  exit-code parity with `rt_c_v_regs--csr` (the bash-harness version).

### F5 - icount counter + trigger in QEMU plugin

- Status: not-started
- Type: behavioral
- Description: Add `uint64_t icount_retired` incremented in a per-insn
  callback registered alongside the CSR/PC matcher in
  `vcpu_tb_trans()`. New plugin arg `icount=N`. When
  `icount_retired >= icount_target`, set the should-exit flag and call
  `on_handoff` at the same insn boundary.
- Key files:
  - `qemu_plugin/hybrid_handoff.c:264-300` (`vcpu_tb_trans`)
  - `qemu_plugin/hybrid_handoff.c:302-339` (arg parser)
- Dependencies: F1
- TDD RED: `tests/hybrid/test_plugin_icount_trigger.sh` runs a fixture
  with `--plugin-args icount=100000` on a CSR-less binary; asserts the
  drained state file's `minstret >= 100000` and exit code is 200. Fails
  because the `icount=` arg is unknown.

### F6 - vsim IcountTrigger (sibling of CsrTrigger)

- Status: not-started
- Type: behavioral
- Description: New `hybrid_vsim/src/hybrid/icount_trigger.hpp` with the
  same duck-type surface as `CsrTrigger` (`lookup(pc) ->
  std::optional<hit>`, plus an `observe_commit()` that increments the
  per-hart counter). `HandoffController` is already template-parameterised
  on trigger type (`handoff_controller.hpp:38`). Add
  `--hybrid-icount N` to `main.cpp:466-472`. Multi-hart out of scope
  (plan-validation rejects `hart_id != 0`).
- Key files:
  - `hybrid_vsim/src/hybrid/icount_trigger.hpp` (new)
  - `hybrid_vsim/src/main.cpp:466-472`
  - `hybrid_vsim/src/simulator.hpp:223-253` (registration)
- Dependencies: none (parallel to F5)
- TDD RED: `hybrid_vsim/tests/hybrid/test_icount_trigger.cpp` (doctest)
  builds a `RecordingBus` + `IcountTrigger(N=100)`, drives 100 retire
  ticks, asserts `on_retire` returns a drained state at exactly the
  100th. Fails because the class does not exist.

### F7 - BBV emission plugin (profile mode)

- Status: not-started
- Type: behavioral
- Description: BBV emission as a second sub-mode of `hybrid_handoff`
  (not a separate plugin - keeps per-insn callback cost to one
  per insn). New plugin args `bbv_out=PATH,slice=N`. Emit one BBV row
  per `slice` dynamic insns into a SimPoint-readable `.bb` file.
- Key files:
  - `qemu_plugin/hybrid_handoff.c`
  - `qemu_plugin/bbv_emit.c` (new sub-module)
  - `qemu_plugin/Makefile`
- Dependencies: F5 (reuses the icount counter)
- TDD RED: `tests/hybrid/test_bbv_emit.sh` runs a fixture with
  `bbv_out=/tmp/x.bb,slice=10000`; asserts the file is SimPoint-format
  and has the correct number of slice rows for the binary's dynamic
  insn count.

### F8 - Orchestrator N-way switching with YAML plan grammar

- Status: not-started
- Type: behavioral
- Description: Parse the full `steps:` grammar. Implement the spawn
  loop: spawn, wait, validate state file, archive (no-op if no
  `archive_as`), advance. Pass `--state-in` from previous spawn's
  `--state-out`. Mmap fd held open across all spawns.
- Key files:
  - `tools/orchestrator/plan.py`
  - `tools/orchestrator/main.py`
  - `tools/orchestrator/spawn.py`
  - `tools/orchestrator/mmap_owner.py` (new)
- Dependencies: F4
- TDD RED: `tools/orchestrator/tests/test_two_step_session.py` runs a
  2-step plan (QEMU -> vsim -> QEMU) on `rt_c_v_regs`; asserts both
  intermediate state files validate. Fails until the loop exists.

### F9 - Checkpoint archive write

- Status: not-started
- Type: behavioral
- Description: When a step has `archive_as: snap/N`, after the spawn
  exits, copy `state.bin` and the mmap into `snap/N/`; write
  `meta.json`. Reflink-or-copy by filesystem probe (btrfs/xfs use
  `cp --reflink=always`; ext4 falls back to sequential `cp`).
- Key files:
  - `tools/orchestrator/checkpoint.py` (new)
  - `tools/orchestrator/main.py`
- Dependencies: F8
- TDD RED:
  `tools/orchestrator/tests/test_checkpoint_archive.py` runs a 1-step
  plan with `archive_as: snap/0`; asserts the three artefacts exist
  and `state.bin` byte-matches the in-flight one.

### F10 - Checkpoint replay (`replay-checkpoint <dir>`)

- Status: not-started
- Type: behavioral
- Description: New orchestrator entry point. Validates `meta.json`
  against the live binary's CPU variant and VLEN. Hydrates a new
  `/dev/shm` mmap from `snap/N/mem`. Copies `state.bin` to working
  path. Spawns the requested model. No plugin or vsim code change.
- Key files:
  - `tools/orchestrator/checkpoint.py`
  - `tools/orchestrator/main.py`
- Dependencies: F9
- TDD RED:
  `tools/orchestrator/tests/test_checkpoint_replay.py` writes a known
  archive (using F9), runs `replay-checkpoint snap/0 --resume-with
  vsim`; asserts the spawned vsim drains a state file whose GPRs
  match the archive.

### F11 - BBV slice-consume trigger

- Status: not-started
- Type: behavioral
- Description: Third sub-mode of `hybrid_handoff` (after CSR/PC,
  icount, BBV-emit): read a slice-index list, fire drain when the
  icount counter hits each slice index. Orchestrator chains N
  invocations through the plan grammar to produce K archived
  regions per SimPoint cluster.
- Key files:
  - `qemu_plugin/hybrid_handoff.c`
  - `qemu_plugin/bbv_emit.c`
  - `tools/orchestrator/plan.py` (new trigger kind `phase`)
- Dependencies: F5, F7, F8
- TDD RED: `tests/hybrid/test_plugin_phase_consume.sh` supplies a
  3-slice list; asserts the plugin drains at exactly the third slice
  (verified by the state file's `minstret`).
```

## Out of Scope (deferred)

- Multi-hart trigger support (per user decision; would be a follow-on
  feature wave). Plan-validation explicitly rejects `hart_id != 0` in F8.
- Snapshot auto-pruning (per user decision; manual disk management
  acceptable in v1).
- Guest-driven cancel (spec open question 5; defer indefinitely).
- KVM/HVF-accelerated QEMU (spec section 5 row 9; plugin API does not
  intercept).
- Cross-ISA switching (spec section 5 row 11; different design problem).

## Approval Required For

This plan covers (a) writing the design above and (b) creating
`/home/nick/work/hybrid_sim/ROADMAP.md` with the exact content shown
in the section above. No source code, build configs, or submodule
files are modified in this session.

file.content:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# F1 contract: QEMU plugin exits with code 200 on drain (matches vsim's
# kExitHandoffToQemu). 0 is reserved for "workload completed normally".
# The orchestrator (F2+) uses this distinction to decide the next spawn.
#
# Required environment (auto-detected with sensible defaults):
#   VSIM_QEMU    qemu-system-riscv64
#   VSIM_PLUGIN  libhybrid_handoff.so
#   VSIM_ELF     a CSR-trigger fixture (default: handoff_roundtrip.elf)

set -euo pipefail

SCRIPT_DIR=$(cd -- "$(dirname -- "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)
REPO_ROOT=$(cd -- "$SCRIPT_DIR/../.." && pwd)

VSIM_QEMU="${VSIM_QEMU:-/local/nick/qemu_v5/build/qemu-system-riscv64}"
VSIM_PLUGIN="${VSIM_PLUGIN:-$REPO_ROOT/hybrid/qemu_plugin/libhybrid_handoff.so}"
VSIM_ELF="${VSIM_ELF:-$REPO_ROOT/hybrid/test/handoff_roundtrip.elf}"

for f in "$VSIM_QEMU" "$VSIM_PLUGIN" "$VSIM_ELF"; do
  if [ ! -e "$f" ]; then
    echo "skip: $f not built"
    exit 77
  fi
done

STATE_QEMU=$(mktemp)
QEMU_LOG=$(mktemp)
trap 'rm -f "$STATE_QEMU" "$QEMU_LOG"' EXIT

set +e
"$VSIM_QEMU" -M virt -cpu andes-ax45mpv,vlen=512 -bios none -nographic \
  -semihosting-config enable=on,target=native \
  -kernel "$VSIM_ELF" \
  -plugin "$VSIM_PLUGIN,outfile=$STATE_QEMU" >"$QEMU_LOG" 2>&1
RC=$?
set -e

if [ "$RC" -ne 200 ]; then
  echo "FAIL[plugin_drain_exit_code]: expected QEMU exit=200 on drain, got $RC"
  echo "--- qemu log ---"
  cat "$QEMU_LOG"
  exit 1
fi

if [ ! -s "$STATE_QEMU" ]; then
  echo "FAIL[plugin_drain_exit_code]: state file is empty"
  exit 1
fi

echo "[plugin_drain_exit_code] PASS: QEMU exit=$RC, state size=$(wc -c <"$STATE_QEMU") bytes"

      LABELS "hybrid-e2e"

  # Contract test: QEMU plugin exits 200 on drain (matches vsim's
  # kExitHandoffToQemu). Guards the F1 rebrand from regressing back to
  # exit(0), which would make workload-completion indistinguishable from
  # a drained handoff under the orchestrator.
  add_test(NAME test_plugin_drain_exit_code
    COMMAND bash ../tests/hybrid/test_plugin_drain_exit_code.sh)
  set_tests_properties(test_plugin_drain_exit_code PROPERTIES
    SKIP_RETURN_CODE 77
    LABELS "hybrid-e2e"
  )
endif()

input.content:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# F5 contract: QEMU plugin icount-trigger mode. With plugin arg `icount=N`
# the plugin drains and exits 200 after N retired insns - even on a binary
# that never executes the magic CSR. Foundation for SimPoint slice-consume
# (F11) and the orchestrator's icount-based switching (F8).
#
# Test binary: spin.elf (li/li/add/j 1b) - no CSR trigger, so today's
# plugin runs forever; the new icount path must fire the drain.
#
# Required environment (auto-detected with sensible defaults):
#   VSIM_QEMU    qemu-system-riscv64
#   VSIM_PLUGIN  libhybrid_handoff.so
#   VSIM_ELF     a CSR-less fixture (default: spin.elf)

set -euo pipefail

SCRIPT_DIR=$(cd -- "$(dirname -- "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)
REPO_ROOT=$(cd -- "$SCRIPT_DIR/../.." && pwd)

VSIM_QEMU="${VSIM_QEMU:-/local/nick/qemu_v5/build/qemu-system-riscv64}"
VSIM_PLUGIN="${VSIM_PLUGIN:-$REPO_ROOT/hybrid/qemu_plugin/libhybrid_handoff.so}"
VSIM_ELF="${VSIM_ELF:-$REPO_ROOT/hybrid/test/spin.elf}"

for f in "$VSIM_QEMU" "$VSIM_PLUGIN" "$VSIM_ELF"; do
  if [ ! -e "$f" ]; then
    echo "skip: $f not built"
    exit 77
  fi
done

ICOUNT_TARGET=100000
STATE_QEMU=$(mktemp)
QEMU_LOG=$(mktemp)
trap 'rm -f "$STATE_QEMU" "$QEMU_LOG"' EXIT

set +e
timeout 30 "$VSIM_QEMU" -M virt -cpu andes-ax45mpv,vlen=512 -bios none -nographic \
  -semihosting-config enable=on,target=native \
  -kernel "$VSIM_ELF" \
  -plugin "$VSIM_PLUGIN,outfile=$STATE_QEMU,icount=$ICOUNT_TARGET" \
  >"$QEMU_LOG" 2>&1
RC=$?
set -e

if [ "$RC" -ne 200 ]; then
  echo "FAIL[plugin_icount_trigger]: expected QEMU exit=200 on drain, got $RC"
  echo "--- qemu log ---"
  cat "$QEMU_LOG"
  exit 1
fi

if [ ! -s "$STATE_QEMU" ]; then
  echo "FAIL[plugin_icount_trigger]: state file is empty"
  exit 1
fi

# minstret lives at offset 632 (state_abi_check.h:35), little-endian uint64.
MINSTRET=$(python3 -c "
import struct, sys
with open('$STATE_QEMU', 'rb') as f:
    f.seek(632)
    print(struct.unpack('<Q', f.read(8))[0])
")

if [ "$MINSTRET" -lt "$ICOUNT_TARGET" ]; then
  echo "FAIL[plugin_icount_trigger]: minstret=$MINSTRET < icount=$ICOUNT_TARGET"
  echo "--- qemu log ---"
  cat "$QEMU_LOG"
  exit 1
fi

echo "[plugin_icount_trigger] PASS: QEMU exit=$RC, minstret=$MINSTRET (target=$ICOUNT_TARGET)"

1	// Translation-time capture of a handoff trigger insn. The QEMU TCG plugin
2	// allocates one of these per matched insn during vcpu_tb_trans and forwards
3	// the pointer as exec-callback userdata. compute_handoff_pc(site) returns
4	// the PC at which vsim should resume (i.e. the architectural PC immediately
5	// after the trigger insn retires).
6	//
7	// This indirection exists because qemu_plugin_read_register("pc") inside an
8	// exec callback returns a stale PC when V instructions retire just before
9	// the trigger -- TCG defers PC writes for performance and the gdbstub
10	// readback observes the lag. vaddr/size are deterministic at translate time
11	// (qemu_plugin_insn_vaddr/size), so vaddr+size is a lag-free post-trigger
12	// PC. Same predicate, two consumers: the plugin (C) and a doctest unit
13	// test (C++).
14	//
15	// Lifetime: sites are heap-allocated in vcpu_tb_trans and never freed --
16	// the plugin has no "TB invalidated" hook and trigger sites are bounded by
17	// the fixture's text (typically 1-2 csrwi insns).
18	
19	#ifndef HYBRID_HANDOFF_SITE_H
20	#define HYBRID_HANDOFF_SITE_H
21	
22	#include "hybrid/insn_match.h"
23	
24	#include <stdint.h>
25	
26	#ifdef __cplusplus
27	extern "C" {
28	#endif
29	
30	typedef struct HandoffSite
31	{
32	  hybrid_handoff_kind kind;
33	  uint64_t            vaddr;
34	  uint32_t            size;
35	} HandoffSite;
36	
37	static inline uint64_t compute_handoff_pc(const HandoffSite* site)
38	{
39	  return site->vaddr + (uint64_t) site->size;
40	}
41	
42	#ifdef __cplusplus
43	}  // extern "C"
44	#endif
45	
46	#endif  // HYBRID_HANDOFF_SITE_H
47

lines: +    // icount-trigger mode: per-vcpu retired-insn counter, incremented in
+    // on_icount_tick. When icount_target is reached, the same drain path
+    // as CSR/PC mode fires (on_handoff -> write_state_atomic -> exit 200).
+// icount-trigger mode: when icount_target is non-zero (set via plugin arg
+// `icount=N`), every translated insn gets an exec callback that bumps a
+// per-vcpu counter; on threshold-cross the drain fires at that insn
+// boundary. Coexists with CSR/PC modes - either trigger can win.

1	// End-to-end roundtrip fixture:
2	//   1. QEMU + plugin sees `csrwi 0x7C0, 0` -> drains state, exits.
3	//   2. vsim resumes, computes a2=3.
4	//   3. vsim sees `csrwi 0x7C0, 1` -> drains state, exits 200.
5	//   4. main spawns qemu-system -gdb -S -semihosting; resume restores GPRs.
6	//   5. QEMU runs to the semihosting EXIT_EXTENDED triple and exits with a1=3.
7	
8	#include "semihost_exit.inc"
9	
10	.section .text
11	.globl _start
12	.globl _hybrid_enter_pc
13	.globl _hybrid_exit_pc
14	_start:
15	_hybrid_enter_pc:
16	    csrwi   0x7C0, 0          // entry: QEMU drains, exits (CSR mode)
17	                              //        OR PC drain at _hybrid_enter_pc (PC mode)
18	    li      a0, 1
19	    li      a1, 2
20	    add     a2, a0, a1        // a2 = 3, computed in vsim only
21	_hybrid_exit_pc:
22	    csrwi   0x7C0, 1          // exit: vsim drains, exit 200
23	    SEMIHOST_EXIT a2
24

file.content:
echo "[$FIXTURE 1/3 mode=$TRIGGER_MODE] QEMU 1 (shared mmap): drain entry state"
# -cpu andes-ax45mpv,vlen=512: gates Andes-specific CSRs (mcache_ctl,
# mmisc_ctl, ...) and pins VLEN to 512 (vlenb=64) so the V state in the
# drain file matches HYBRID_VLEN_BYTES_MAX. Non-V fixtures run identically
# under either VLEN.
# -semihosting-config enable=on,target=native: lets fixtures print from
# QEMU1 (phase 1) via newlib stdio (linked against libgloss_vh), matching
# the QEMU2 launch in qemu_handback.hpp. Phase 2 (vsim) is forbidden from
# doing any syscall -- vsim does not implement semihosting.
# QEMU1 plugin drains state then exits 200 (== kExitHandoffToQemu) to
# distinguish drain from workload completion (0). See F1 in ROADMAP.md.
set +e
"$VSIM_QEMU" \
  -object "memory-backend-file,id=mem,share=on,mem-path=$SHARED_MEM,size=$SHARED_SIZE" \
  -machine andes_ae350,memory-backend=mem \
  -cpu andes-ax45mpv,vlen=512 \
  -nographic \
  -semihosting-config enable=on,target=native \
  -bios "$VSIM_ELF" \
  -plugin "$VSIM_PLUGIN,$PLUGIN_ARGS" >/dev/null 2>&1
QEMU1_RC=$?
set -e

if [ "$QEMU1_RC" -ne 200 ]; then
  echo "FAIL[$FIXTURE]: QEMU 1 expected exit 200 on drain, got $QEMU1_RC"
  exit 1
fi

if [ ! -s "$STATE_QEMU" ]; then
  echo "FAIL[$FIXTURE]: QEMU 1 did not produce state"
  exit 1
fi

echo "[$FIXTURE 2/3 mode=$TRIGGER_MODE] vsim (shared mmap): resume + drain + spawn QEMU 2"

1	#!/usr/bin/env bash
2	# Tier-2 round-trip e2e: cross-simulator memory survival via shared mmap.
3	#
4	# QEMU 1, vsim, and QEMU 2 all map the same backing file. Phase A writes a
5	# pattern in QEMU 1; Phase B verifies + mutates in vsim; Phase C re-reads
6	# in QEMU 2. Memory must survive both handoffs.
7	#
8	# Required environment:
9	#   VSIM_BIN     path to sim_<cpu> binary (host-runnable)
10	#   VSIM_QEMU    path to qemu-system-riscv64
11	#   VSIM_PLUGIN  path to libhybrid_handoff.so
12	
13	set -euo pipefail
14	
15	SCRIPT_DIR=$(cd -- "$(dirname -- "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)
16	REPO_ROOT=$(cd -- "$SCRIPT_DIR/../.." && pwd)
17	
18	# Fixture-parameterized: defaults match the original rt_shared_mem.S/elf
19	# fixture, but C-level Tier-2 fixtures (rt_c_hello, rt_c_printf, ...)
20	# reuse this harness by overriding FIXTURE/EXPECT_RC.
21	FIXTURE="${FIXTURE:-rt_shared_mem}"
22	EXPECT_RC="${EXPECT_RC:-0}"
23	TIMEOUT="${TIMEOUT:-60}"
24	TRIGGER_MODE="${TRIGGER_MODE:-csr}"
25	
26	VSIM_BIN="${VSIM_BIN:-$REPO_ROOT/build/sim_ax45mpv_premium}"
27	VSIM_QEMU="${VSIM_QEMU:-/local/nick/qemu_v5/build/qemu-system-riscv64}"
28	VSIM_PLUGIN="${VSIM_PLUGIN:-$REPO_ROOT/hybrid/qemu_plugin/libhybrid_handoff.so}"
29	VSIM_ELF="${VSIM_ELF:-$REPO_ROOT/hybrid/test/${FIXTURE}.elf}"
30	NM="${NM:-/local/nick/SW_Release/build-ast540/build-toolchain/linux/nds64le-elf-newlib-v5d/bin/riscv64-unknown-elf-nm}"
31	
32	for f in "$VSIM_BIN" "$VSIM_QEMU" "$VSIM_PLUGIN" "$VSIM_ELF"; do
33	  if [ ! -e "$f" ]; then
34	    echo "skip: $f not built; run 'make' under hybrid/test and hybrid/qemu_plugin"
35	    exit 77
36	  fi
37	done
38	
39	case "$TRIGGER_MODE" in
40	  csr) ;;
41	  pc)
42	    if ! command -v "$NM" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
43	      echo "skip[$FIXTURE pc]: nm not found at $NM"
44	      exit 77
45	    fi
46	    # Capture nm output once: avoids racing awk's `exit` against nm's pipe
47	    # writes (large C-fixture symbol tables make nm SIGPIPE under pipefail).
48	    NM_OUT=$("$NM" --defined-only "$VSIM_ELF")
49	    ENTER_PC=$(printf '%s\n' "$NM_OUT" \
50	               | awk '$3=="_hybrid_enter_pc"{print $1}')
51	    EXIT_PC=$(printf '%s\n' "$NM_OUT" \
52	              | awk '$3=="_hybrid_exit_pc"{print $1}')
53	    if [ -z "${ENTER_PC:-}" ] || [ -z "${EXIT_PC:-}" ]; then
54	      echo "FAIL[$FIXTURE pc]: missing _hybrid_enter_pc/_hybrid_exit_pc symbols"
55	      exit 1
56	    fi
57	    ;;
58	  *)
59	    echo "FAIL[$FIXTURE]: unknown TRIGGER_MODE=$TRIGGER_MODE (use csr|pc)"
60	    exit 1
61	    ;;
62	esac
63	
64	# 128 MB matches the QEMU virt machine default; vsim must use the same size.
65	SHARED_SIZE=$((128 * 1024 * 1024))
66	SHARED_MEM=$(mktemp -p /dev/shm vsim-rt-shared.XXXXXX)
67	STATE_QEMU=$(mktemp)
68	STATE_VSIM=$(mktemp)
69	SIM_LOG=$(mktemp)
70	trap 'rm -f "$SHARED_MEM" "$STATE_QEMU" "$STATE_VSIM" "$SIM_LOG"' EXIT
71	
72	truncate -s "$SHARED_SIZE" "$SHARED_MEM"
73	
74	PLUGIN_ARGS="outfile=$STATE_QEMU"
75	VSIM_TRIG_ARGS=()
76	if [ "$TRIGGER_MODE" = "pc" ]; then
77	  PLUGIN_ARGS="$PLUGIN_ARGS,enter_pc=0x$ENTER_PC"
78	  VSIM_TRIG_ARGS=(--hybrid-exit-pc "0x$EXIT_PC")
79	fi
80

file.content:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Tier-2 round-trip e2e: cross-simulator memory survival via shared mmap.
#
# QEMU 1, vsim, and QEMU 2 all map the same backing file. Phase A writes a
# pattern in QEMU 1; Phase B verifies + mutates in vsim; Phase C re-reads
# in QEMU 2. Memory must survive both handoffs.
#
# Required environment:
#   VSIM_BIN     path to sim_<cpu> binary (host-runnable)
#   VSIM_QEMU    path to qemu-system-riscv64
#   VSIM_PLUGIN  path to libhybrid_handoff.so

set -euo pipefail

SCRIPT_DIR=$(cd -- "$(dirname -- "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)
REPO_ROOT=$(cd -- "$SCRIPT_DIR/../.." && pwd)

# Fixture-parameterized: defaults match the original rt_shared_mem.S/elf
# fixture, but C-level Tier-2 fixtures (rt_c_hello, rt_c_printf, ...)
# reuse this harness by overriding FIXTURE/EXPECT_RC.
FIXTURE="${FIXTURE:-rt_shared_mem}"
EXPECT_RC="${EXPECT_RC:-0}"
TIMEOUT="${TIMEOUT:-60}"
TRIGGER_MODE="${TRIGGER_MODE:-csr}"

VSIM_BIN="${VSIM_BIN:-$REPO_ROOT/build/sim_ax45mpv_premium}"
VSIM_QEMU="${VSIM_QEMU:-/local/nick/qemu_v5/build/qemu-system-riscv64}"
VSIM_PLUGIN="${VSIM_PLUGIN:-$REPO_ROOT/hybrid/qemu_plugin/libhybrid_handoff.so}"
VSIM_ELF="${VSIM_ELF:-$REPO_ROOT/hybrid/test/${FIXTURE}.elf}"
NM="${NM:-/local/nick/SW_Release/build-ast540/build-toolchain/linux/nds64le-elf-newlib-v5d/bin/riscv64-unknown-elf-nm}"

for f in "$VSIM_BIN" "$VSIM_QEMU" "$VSIM_PLUGIN" "$VSIM_ELF"; do
  if [ ! -e "$f" ]; then
    echo "skip: $f not built; run 'make' under hybrid/test and hybrid/qemu_plugin"
    exit 77
  fi
done

case "$TRIGGER_MODE" in
  csr) ;;
  pc)
    if ! command -v "$NM" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
      echo "skip[$FIXTURE pc]: nm not found at $NM"
      exit 77
    fi
    # Capture nm output once: avoids racing awk's `exit` against nm's pipe
    # writes (large C-fixture symbol tables make nm SIGPIPE under pipefail).
    NM_OUT=$("$NM" --defined-only "$VSIM_ELF")
    ENTER_PC=$(printf '%s\n' "$NM_OUT" \
               | awk '$3=="_hybrid_enter_pc"{print $1}')
    EXIT_PC=$(printf '%s\n' "$NM_OUT" \
              | awk '$3=="_hybrid_exit_pc"{print $1}')
    if [ -z "${ENTER_PC:-}" ] || [ -z "${EXIT_PC:-}" ]; then
      echo "FAIL[$FIXTURE pc]: missing _hybrid_enter_pc/_hybrid_exit_pc symbols"
      exit 1
    fi
    ;;
  *)
    echo "FAIL[$FIXTURE]: unknown TRIGGER_MODE=$TRIGGER_MODE (use csr|pc)"
    exit 1
    ;;
esac

# 128 MB matches the QEMU virt machine default; vsim must use the same size.
SHARED_SIZE=$((128 * 1024 * 1024))
SHARED_MEM=$(mktemp -p /dev/shm vsim-rt-shared.XXXXXX)
STATE_QEMU=$(mktemp)
STATE_VSIM=$(mktemp)
SIM_LOG=$(mktemp)
trap 'rm -f "$SHARED_MEM" "$STATE_QEMU" "$STATE_VSIM" "$SIM_LOG"' EXIT

truncate -s "$SHARED_SIZE" "$SHARED_MEM"

PLUGIN_ARGS="outfile=$STATE_QEMU"
VSIM_TRIG_ARGS=()
if [ "$TRIGGER_MODE" = "pc" ]; then
  PLUGIN_ARGS="$PLUGIN_ARGS,enter_pc=0x$ENTER_PC"
  VSIM_TRIG_ARGS=(--hybrid-exit-pc "0x$EXIT_PC")
fi

  # Tier-2: separate driver because it allocates /dev/shm and uses
  # --shared-mem-path / -object memory-backend-file to share RAM across

  # Format: fname:expected_rc[:timeout_s]. Default timeout is 60 (set in
  # rt_shared_mem.sh). rt_c_matmul needs more headroom because the GEMM
  # kernel runs cycle-accurately under vsim (~45-50s) before drain.
  set(VSIM_RT_T2_FIXTURES
    "rt_shared_mem:0"
    # C-level fixtures: linked against vsim-demo C runtime (crt0/trap/libgloss/
    # handler). They need Tier-2 (shared mmap) so the stack and heap survive
    # the vsim->QEMU2 boundary -- main()'s ra-spill/restore around the
    # csrwi 0x7C0,1 marker would otherwise read uninitialized RAM in QEMU2.
    "rt_c_hello:0"
    "rt_c_printf:0"
    "rt_c_matmul:0:120"
    # Regression for the "no redundant ELF reload" rule: .bss survival
    # across QEMU1 -> vsim -> QEMU2 without users hand-allocating fixed
    # addresses. Fails today because vsim's elf_loader and QEMU 2's
    # -kernel both zero-fill the .bss tail of every PT_LOAD.
    "rt_c_static_buf:0"
    # 1D 3-tap FIR convolution as a real RVV use case spanning the
    # handoff: QEMU1 generates x via intrinsics; vsim applies the kernel
    # (vslide1up / vslide1down + vadd) using x carried in its compiler-
    # picked V register; QEMU2 stores y and scalar-verifies. V regfile
    # transport rides on the save-all / reload-all helpers in
    # runtime/v_regfile_xfer.{h,c} so the fixture body is pure intrinsics
    # with no physical V register references.
    "rt_c_v_regs:0"
    # V scalar CSR survival: sets vxsat/vxrm/vtype/vl/vstart in vsim phase,
    # verifies in QEMU2. Exercises the full Tier-A+B restore path.
    "rt_c_v_csrs:0"
    # 16x16x16 RVV GEMM under vsim, scalar reference recompute under QEMU 2.
    # Same cycle-accurate budget headroom as rt_c_matmul (the V kernel runs
    # fewer instructions but issue/execute is implementation-dependent).
    "rt_c_v_matmul:0:120"
  )
  foreach(entry ${VSIM_RT_T2_FIXTURES})
    string(REPLACE ":" ";" parts ${entry})
    list(GET parts 0 fname)
    list(GET parts 1 erc)
    list(LENGTH parts nparts)
    set(timeout_env "")
    set(tcost 60)
    if(nparts GREATER 2)
      list(GET parts 2 tsec)
      set(timeout_env "TIMEOUT=${tsec} ")
      # Use the per-fixture timeout as the scheduling cost so long
      # fixtures (rt_c_matmul: 120s) start in the first wave under -j.
      set(tcost ${tsec})
    endif()
    # CSR variant.
    add_test(
      NAME test_e2e_${fname}
      COMMAND bash -c
        "${timeout_env}TRIGGER_MODE=csr FIXTURE=${fname} EXPECT_RC=${erc} bash ../tests/hybrid/rt_shared_mem.sh"
    )
    set_tests_properties(test_e2e_${fname} PROPERTIES
      SKIP_RETURN_CODE 77
      LABELS "hybrid-e2e;hybrid-e2e-csr"
      COST ${tcost}
    )
    # PC variant: same fixture, same timeout, mode flipped.
    add_test(
      NAME test_e2e_${fname}_pc
      COMMAND bash -c
        "${timeout_env}TRIGGER_MODE=pc FIXTURE=${fname} EXPECT_RC=${erc} bash ../tests/hybrid/rt_shared_mem.sh"
    )
    set_tests_properties(test_e2e_${fname}_pc PROPERTIES
      SKIP_RETURN_CODE 77
      LABELS "hybrid-e2e;hybrid-e2e-pc"
      COST ${tcost}
    )
  endforeach()

  # Negative / robustness drivers: each guards a quietly-degrading failure
  # path the happy-path round-trips can't see (corrupted state file,
  # missing --qemu-binary, silent gdbstub peer, tty-induced SIGTTOU).
  # No PC variants - these assert orchestrator failure modes that are
  # orthogonal to the trigger mechanism.
  foreach(neg corrupt_state no_qemu_binary gdbstub_timeout pty_handback)
    add_test(NAME test_e2e_rt_${neg}
      COMMAND bash ../tests/hybrid/rt_${neg}.sh)
    set_tests_properties(test_e2e_rt_${neg} PROPERTIES
      SKIP_RETURN_CODE 77

file.content:
-nodefaultlibs -I$(RUNTIME_DIR) -Wall

.PHONY: all clean dump

all: spin.elf handoff.elf handoff_exit.elf handoff_roundtrip.elf \
     $(RT_FIXTURES:%=%.elf) $(RT_C_FIXTURES:%=%.elf) \
     $(RT_C_V_FIXTURES:%=%.elf) \
     $(PROBE_V_FIXTURES:%=%.elf)

spin.elf: spin.S spin.ld
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c spin.S -o spin.o
	$(LD) $(LDFLAGS) spin.o -o $@

handoff.elf: handoff.S handoff.ld
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c handoff.S -o handoff.o
	$(LD) -T handoff.ld -nostdlib -static handoff.o -o $@

handoff_exit.elf: handoff_exit.S handoff_exit.ld
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c handoff_exit.S -o handoff_exit.o
	$(LD) -T handoff_exit.ld -nostdlib -static handoff_exit.o -o $@

handoff_roundtrip.elf: handoff_roundtrip.S handoff_roundtrip.ld semihost_exit.inc
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c handoff_roundtrip.S -o handoff_roundtrip.o
	$(LD) -T handoff_roundtrip.ld -nostdlib -static handoff_roundtrip.o -o $@

$(RT_FIXTURES:%=%.elf): %.elf: %.S handoff_roundtrip.ld semihost_exit.inc
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c $< -o $*.o
	$(LD) -T handoff_roundtrip.ld -nostdlib -static $*.o -o $@

# C-level fixtures: compile+link via gcc (newlib pulled). crt0.S is
# passed FIRST so _start lands at the load address (0x80000000) -- ld
# preserves input order within the .text wildcard.
$(RT_C_FIXTURES:%=%.elf): %.elf: %.c $(RUNTIME_SRCS) $(RUNTIME_DIR)/rt_c.ld $(RUNTIME_DIR)/rt_c_helpers.h
	$(CC) $(RT_C_CFLAGS) -T $(RUNTIME_DIR)/rt_c.ld \
	  $(RUNTIME_SRCS) $< $(RT_C_LIBS) -o $@

# RVV-enabled fixtures: same runtime + v_regfile_xfer.c, different
# -march. Listed separately so changes to RT_C_CFLAGS don't accidentally
# drop V.
$(RT_C_V_FIXTURES:%=%.elf): %.elf: %.c $(RT_C_V_RUNTIME_SRCS) $(RUNTIME_DIR)/rt_c.ld $(RUNTIME_DIR)/rt_c_helpers.h $(RUNTIME_DIR)/v_regfile_xfer.h

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# Build tiny RV64 fixtures used by hybrid smoke tests.
# Default toolchain matches /local/nick/SW_Release/build-ast540/...

TOOLCHAIN ?= /local/nick/SW_Release/build-ast540/build-toolchain/linux/nds64le-elf-newlib-v5d
CC        := $(TOOLCHAIN)/bin/riscv64-unknown-elf-gcc
LD        := $(TOOLCHAIN)/bin/riscv64-unknown-elf-ld
OBJDUMP   := $(TOOLCHAIN)/bin/riscv64-unknown-elf-objdump

CFLAGS  := -march=rv64gc -mabi=lp64d -nostdlib -static -Wall
LDFLAGS := -T spin.ld -nostdlib -static

# Tier-1 round-trip e2e fixtures share handoff_roundtrip.ld.
RT_FIXTURES := rt_all_gprs rt_abi_aliases rt_sign_bits rt_pc_jump \
               rt_long_roi rt_mem_loadstore rt_unaligned_lh \
               rt_shared_mem rt_csr_survival

# C-level fixtures: standard newlib stdio (printf, fputs, ...) linked
# against the Andes virtual-hosting libgloss (libgloss_vh.a), which
# implements _write / _exit / _sbrk / _read / ... via the RISC-V
# semihosting magic triple. QEMU1 and QEMU2 are launched with
# -semihosting-config enable=on,target=native and intercept the triple
# before it reaches mtvec. vsim does not implement semihosting -- phase
# 2 (the span between enter_vsim and exit_vsim)
# is therefore syscall-free by rule (see runtime/rt_c_helpers.h).
RT_C_FIXTURES    := rt_c_hello rt_c_printf rt_c_matmul rt_c_static_buf
RT_C_V_FIXTURES  := rt_c_v_regs rt_c_v_csrs rt_c_v_matmul
# Probe fixtures: QEMU1-only, no vsim/QEMU2 phase. Used by shell tests
# that inspect the raw state file to verify plugin drain behavior.
PROBE_V_FIXTURES := probe_v_csr

RUNTIME_DIR     := runtime
RUNTIME_SRCS    := $(RUNTIME_DIR)/crt0.S $(RUNTIME_DIR)/trap.S \
                   $(RUNTIME_DIR)/handler.c
# V fixtures additionally need the V regfile boundary helpers
# (v_regfile_reload_all / v_regfile_dump_all). Bodies live in a
# separate TU so the compiler treats each call as opaque and will not
# reorder vector compute across the handoff boundary.
RT_C_V_RUNTIME_SRCS := $(RUNTIME_SRCS) $(RUNTIME_DIR)/v_regfile_xfer.c
# -nodefaultlibs suppresses the toolchain's default -lc/-lgloss/-lgcc;
# we re-add them inside --start-group so -lgloss_vh's semihosting stubs
# resolve newlib's _write / _exit / _sbrk / ... before -lgloss (the
# proxy-kernel ecall variant) is even searched. Order matters:
# -lgloss_vh must precede the fallback -lgloss path.
RT_C_LIBS       := -Wl,--start-group -lc -lgloss_vh -lgcc -Wl,--end-group
RT_C_CFLAGS     := -march=rv64gc -mabi=lp64d -O1 -static -nostartfiles \
                   -nodefaultlibs -I$(RUNTIME_DIR) -Wall
# RVV-using fixtures need -march=rv64gcv. The crt0 already sets
# mstatus.VS to Initial, so V instructions don't trap.
RT_C_V_CFLAGS   := -march=rv64gcv -mabi=lp64d -O1 -static -nostartfiles \
                   -nodefaultlibs -I$(RUNTIME_DIR) -Wall

.PHONY: all clean dump

all: spin.elf handoff.elf handoff_exit.elf handoff_roundtrip.elf \
     $(RT_FIXTURES:%=%.elf) $(RT_C_FIXTURES:%=%.elf) \
     $(RT_C_V_FIXTURES:%=%.elf) \
     $(PROBE_V_FIXTURES:%=%.elf)

spin.elf: spin.S spin.ld
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c spin.S -o spin.o
	$(LD) $(LDFLAGS) spin.o -o $@

handoff.elf: handoff.S handoff.ld
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c handoff.S -o handoff.o
	$(LD) -T handoff.ld -nostdlib -static handoff.o -o $@

handoff_exit.elf: handoff_exit.S handoff_exit.ld
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c handoff_exit.S -o handoff_exit.o
	$(LD) -T handoff_exit.ld -nostdlib -static handoff_exit.o -o $@

handoff_roundtrip.elf: handoff_roundtrip.S handoff_roundtrip.ld semihost_exit.inc
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c handoff_roundtrip.S -o handoff_roundtrip.o
	$(LD) -T handoff_roundtrip.ld -nostdlib -static handoff_roundtrip.o -o $@

$(RT_FIXTURES:%=%.elf): %.elf: %.S handoff_roundtrip.ld semihost_exit.inc
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c $< -o $*.o
	$(LD) -T handoff_roundtrip.ld -nostdlib -static $*.o -o $@

# C-level fixtures: compile+link via gcc (newlib pulled). crt0.S is
# passed FIRST so _start lands at the load address (0x80000000) -- ld
# preserves input order within the .text wildcard.
$(RT_C_FIXTURES:%=%.elf): %.elf: %.c $(RUNTIME_SRCS) $(RUNTIME_DIR)/rt_c.ld $(RUNTIME_DIR)/rt_c_helpers.h
	$(CC) $(RT_C_CFLAGS) -T $(RUNTIME_DIR)/rt_c.ld \
	  $(RUNTIME_SRCS) $< $(RT_C_LIBS) -o $@

# RVV-enabled fixtures: same runtime + v_regfile_xfer.c, different
# -march. Listed separately so changes to RT_C_CFLAGS don't accidentally
# drop V.
$(RT_C_V_FIXTURES:%=%.elf): %.elf: %.c $(RT_C_V_RUNTIME_SRCS) $(RUNTIME_DIR)/rt_c.ld $(RUNTIME_DIR)/rt_c_helpers.h $(RUNTIME_DIR)/v_regfile_xfer.h
	$(CC) $(RT_C_V_CFLAGS) -T $(RUNTIME_DIR)/rt_c.ld \
	  $(RT_C_V_RUNTIME_SRCS) $< $(RT_C_LIBS) -o $@

# Probe fixtures: pure asm, -march=rv64gcv (need V CSR names), no C runtime.
PROBE_V_CFLAGS := -march=rv64gcv -mabi=lp64d -nostdlib -static -Wall
$(PROBE_V_FIXTURES:%=%.elf): %.elf: %.S handoff_roundtrip.ld
	$(CC) $(PROBE_V_CFLAGS) -c $< -o $*.o
	$(LD) -T handoff_roundtrip.ld -nostdlib -static $*.o -o $@

dump: spin.elf handoff.elf handoff_exit.elf
	$(OBJDUMP) -d spin.elf
	$(OBJDUMP) -d handoff.elf
	$(OBJDUMP) -d handoff_exit.elf

clean:
	rm -f spin.o spin.elf handoff.o handoff.elf handoff_exit.o handoff_exit.elf \
	      handoff_roundtrip.o handoff_roundtrip.elf \
	      $(RT_FIXTURES:%=%.o) $(RT_FIXTURES:%=%.elf) \
	      $(RT_C_FIXTURES:%=%.elf) $(RT_C_V_FIXTURES:%=%.elf) \
	      $(PROBE_V_FIXTURES:%=%.o) $(PROBE_V_FIXTURES:%=%.elf)

input.content:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# F5 contract: QEMU plugin icount-trigger mode. With plugin arg `icount=N`
# the plugin drains and exits 200 after N retired insns - even on a binary
# that never executes the magic CSR. Foundation for SimPoint slice-consume
# (F11) and the orchestrator's icount-based switching (F8).
#
# Test binary: icount_spin.elf (no csrwi 0x7C0; linked at 0x80000000 for
# andes_ae350). Today's plugin would run forever; the new icount path
# must fire the drain.
#
# Required environment (auto-detected with sensible defaults):
#   VSIM_QEMU    qemu-system-riscv64
#   VSIM_PLUGIN  libhybrid_handoff.so
#   VSIM_ELF     a CSR-less fixture (default: icount_spin.elf)

set -euo pipefail

SCRIPT_DIR=$(cd -- "$(dirname -- "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)
REPO_ROOT=$(cd -- "$SCRIPT_DIR/../.." && pwd)

VSIM_QEMU="${VSIM_QEMU:-/local/nick/qemu_v5/build/qemu-system-riscv64}"
VSIM_PLUGIN="${VSIM_PLUGIN:-$REPO_ROOT/hybrid/qemu_plugin/libhybrid_handoff.so}"
VSIM_ELF="${VSIM_ELF:-$REPO_ROOT/hybrid/test/icount_spin.elf}"

for f in "$VSIM_QEMU" "$VSIM_PLUGIN" "$VSIM_ELF"; do
  if [ ! -e "$f" ]; then
    echo "skip: $f not built"
    exit 77
  fi
done

ICOUNT_TARGET=100000
SHARED_SIZE=$((128 * 1024 * 1024))
SHARED_MEM=$(mktemp -p /dev/shm vsim-icount-shared.XXXXXX)
STATE_QEMU=$(mktemp)
QEMU_LOG=$(mktemp)
trap 'rm -f "$SHARED_MEM" "$STATE_QEMU" "$QEMU_LOG"' EXIT

truncate -s "$SHARED_SIZE" "$SHARED_MEM"

set +e
timeout 30 "$VSIM_QEMU" \
  -object "memory-backend-file,id=mem,share=on,mem-path=$SHARED_MEM,size=$SHARED_SIZE" \
  -machine andes_ae350,memory-backend=mem \
  -cpu andes-ax45mpv,vlen=512 \
  -nographic \
  -semihosting-config enable=on,target=native \
  -bios "$VSIM_ELF" \
  -plugin "$VSIM_PLUGIN,outfile=$STATE_QEMU,icount=$ICOUNT_TARGET" \
  >"$QEMU_LOG" 2>&1
RC=$?
set -e

if [ "$RC" -ne 200 ]; then
  echo "FAIL[plugin_icount_trigger]: expected QEMU exit=200 on drain, got $RC"
  echo "--- qemu log ---"
  cat "$QEMU_LOG"
  exit 1
fi

if [ ! -s "$STATE_QEMU" ]; then
  echo "FAIL[plugin_icount_trigger]: state file is empty"
  exit 1
fi

# minstret lives at offset 632 (state_abi_check.h:35), little-endian uint64.
MINSTRET=$(python3 -c "
import struct, sys
with open('$STATE_QEMU', 'rb') as f:
    f.seek(632)
    print(struct.unpack('<Q', f.read(8))[0])
")

if [ "$MINSTRET" -lt "$ICOUNT_TARGET" ]; then
  echo "FAIL[plugin_icount_trigger]: minstret=$MINSTRET < icount=$ICOUNT_TARGET"
  echo "--- qemu log ---"
  cat "$QEMU_LOG"
  exit 1
fi

echo "[plugin_icount_trigger] PASS: QEMU exit=$RC, minstret=$MINSTRET (target=$ICOUNT_TARGET)"
